<type>file</type>
<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env node
2: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
3: // MODULE: mk-code-index MCP Server Entrypoint
4: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
5: /**
6:  * mk-code-index MCP server entry point.
7:  *
8:  * ## Environment Variables
9:  *
10:  * - `MK_CODE_INDEX_ROOT_DIR` — Workspace root directory used for readiness
11:  *   snapshot evaluation and the marker JSON body. The marker file path is
12:  *   resolved from the configured code-graph database directory.
13:  */
14: import { Server } from '@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/index.js';
15: import { StdioServerTransport } from '@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/stdio.js';
16: import { CallToolRequestSchema, ListToolsRequestSchema } from '@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/types.js';
17: import * as codeGraphTools from './tools/index.js';
18: import { CODE_GRAPH_TOOL_SCHEMAS } from './tool-schemas.js';
19: import { writeCodeGraphReadinessMarker } from './lib/readiness-marker.js';
20: import { closeDbWithAssertion, refreshOwnerLease } from './lib/index.js';
21: import { DATABASE_DIR } from './core/config.js';
22: import {
23:   getIpcBridgeStats,
24:   resolveIpcSocketPath,
25:   startIpcSocketServer,
26:   type IpcSocketServerHandle,
27: } from './lib/ipc/socket-server.js';
28: import {
29:   createLauncherIdleMonitor,
30:   type LauncherIdleMonitor,
31: } from './lib/ipc/launcher-idle-timeout.js';
32: 
33: const DEFAULT_OWNER_LEASE_TTL_MS = 60_000;
34: const OWNER_LEASE_REFRESH_INTERVAL_MS = DEFAULT_OWNER_LEASE_TTL_MS / 3;
35: let ownerLeaseRefreshTimer: ReturnType<typeof setInterval> | null = null;
36: let ownerLeaseMismatchShutdownStarted = false;
37: 
38: function clearOwnerLeaseRefreshTimer(): void {
39:   if (!ownerLeaseRefreshTimer) return;
40:   clearInterval(ownerLeaseRefreshTimer);
41:   ownerLeaseRefreshTimer = null;
42: }
43: 
44: function startOwnerLeaseRefreshTimer(): void {
45:   if (ownerLeaseRefreshTimer) return;
46:   ownerLeaseRefreshTimer = setInterval(() => {
47:     try {
48:       const refreshed = refreshOwnerLease(DATABASE_DIR, process.pid);
49:       if (!refreshed && !ownerLeaseMismatchShutdownStarted) {
50:         ownerLeaseMismatchShutdownStarted = true;
51:         void shutdownCodeIndex('owner lease moved to another process').finally(() => process.exit(0));
52:       }
53:     } catch (error: unknown) {
54:       const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
55:       console.error(`[owner-lease] heartbeat refresh failed: ${message}`);
56:     }
57:   }, OWNER_LEASE_REFRESH_INTERVAL_MS);
58:   ownerLeaseRefreshTimer.unref();
59: }
60: 
61: process.on('uncaughtException', (err) => {
62:   console.error('[mk-code-index] uncaughtException:', err);
63:   clearOwnerLeaseRefreshTimer();
64:   closeDbWithAssertion();
65:   process.exit(1);
66: });
67: process.on('unhandledRejection', (reason) => {
68:   console.error('[mk-code-index] unhandledRejection:', reason);
69:   clearOwnerLeaseRefreshTimer();
70:   closeDbWithAssertion();
71:   process.exit(1);
72: });
73: 
74: function createCodeIndexMcpServer(): Server {
75:   const codeIndexServer = new Server(
76:     { name: 'mk-code-index', version: '1.0.0' },
77:     { capabilities: { tools: {} } }
78:   );
79: 
80:   codeIndexServer.setRequestHandler(ListToolsRequestSchema, async () => ({
81:     tools: CODE_GRAPH_TOOL_SCHEMAS,
82:   }));
83: 
84:   codeIndexServer.setRequestHandler(CallToolRequestSchema, async (request): Promise<any> => {
85:     const args = (request.params.arguments ?? {}) as Record<string, unknown>;
86:     return await codeGraphTools.dispatch(request.params.name, args);
87:   });
88: 
89:   return codeIndexServer;
90: }
91: 
92: let ipcBridge: IpcSocketServerHandle | null = null;
93: let launcherIdleMonitor: LauncherIdleMonitor | null = null;
94: let shutdownPromise: Promise<void> | null = null;
95: 
96: async function shutdownCodeIndex(reason: string): Promise<void> {
97:   if (shutdownPromise) return shutdownPromise;
98: 
99:   shutdownPromise = (async () => {
100:     console.error(`[mk-code-index] ${reason}`);
101:     clearOwnerLeaseRefreshTimer();
102:     if (launcherIdleMonitor) {
103:       launcherIdleMonitor.stop();
104:       launcherIdleMonitor = null;
105:     }
106:     if (ipcBridge) {
107:       const bridge = ipcBridge;
108:       ipcBridge = null;
109:       await bridge.close().catch((error: unknown) => {
110:         const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
111:         console.error(`[mk-code-index] ipc-bridge close error: ${message}`);
112:       });
113:     }
114:     closeDbWithAssertion();
115:   })();
116: 
117:   return shutdownPromise;
118: }
119: 
120: process.once('SIGINT', () => {
121:   void shutdownCodeIndex('SIGINT').finally(() => process.exit(0));
122: });
123: process.once('SIGTERM', () => {
124:   void shutdownCodeIndex('SIGTERM').finally(() => process.exit(0));
125: });
126: 
127: const server = createCodeIndexMcpServer();
128: 
129: try {
130:   writeCodeGraphReadinessMarker(process.env.MK_CODE_INDEX_ROOT_DIR || process.cwd());
131: } catch (error: unknown) {
132:   const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
133:   console.error(`[mk-code-index] readiness marker write failed: ${message}`);
134: }
135: 
136: const transport = new StdioServerTransport();
137: try {
138:   await server.connect(transport);
139:   startOwnerLeaseRefreshTimer();
140:   launcherIdleMonitor = createLauncherIdleMonitor({
141:     serviceName: 'mk-code-index',
142:     getActiveClientCount: () => getIpcBridgeStats().secondary_clients_count,
143:     onIdle: async () => {
144:       await shutdownCodeIndex('launcher idle timeout');
145:       process.exit(0);
146:     },
147:     log: (message: string) => console.error(message),
148:   });
149:   ipcBridge = await startIpcSocketServer({
150:     socketPath: resolveIpcSocketPath(DATABASE_DIR),
151:     createServer: () => createCodeIndexMcpServer(),
152:     log: (message: string) => console.error(message),
153:     onActivity: () => launcherIdleMonitor?.markActivity(),
154:   });
155: } catch (error: unknown) {
156:   clearOwnerLeaseRefreshTimer();
157:   const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
158:   const stack = error instanceof Error ? error.stack : undefined;
159:   console.error('[mk-code-index] connect failed:', message);
160:   if (stack) console.error(stack);
161:   process.exit(1);
162: }

(End of file - total 162 lines)
</content>

Token Budget: 500.',

126:   inputSchema: {
127:     type: 'object', additionalProperties: false,
128:     properties: {

146: const codeGraphApply: ToolDefinition = {
147:   name: 'code_graph_apply',
148:   description: '[L8:Code Graph] Verification-gated apply-mode for code graph recovery. Runs the gold-query battery before and after typed recovery operations, enforces soft-stale self-healing boundaries, writes JSONL audit logs, and rolls back bad applies. Token Budget: 1000.',
149:   inputSchema: {
150:     type: 'object', additionalProperties: false,
151:     properties: {
152:       rootDir: { type: 'string', description: 'Workspace root (default: current working directory)' },
153:       operation: {
154:         type: 'string',
155:         enum: ['rescan', 'prune-excludes', 'repair-nodes', 'recover-sqlite-corruption', 'rollback-bad-apply'],
156:         description: 'Apply operation to run. Defaults to re-scan routing based on staleness state.',
157:       },
158:       confirm: { type: 'boolean', description: 'Required for hard-stale recovery before any mutation.' },
159:       dryRun: { type: 'boolean', description: 'Run pre/post batteries and classification, but skip operation dispatch.' },
160:       crashRootCauseAddressed: { type: 'boolean', description: 'Required true before repair-nodes re-parses parser_skip_list candidates.' },
161:       quarantineOlderThanDays: { type: 'number', minimum: 1, maximum: 365, description: 'Minimum parser_skip_list age for repair-nodes eligibility.' },
162:       lowTierOptIn: { type: 'boolean', description: 'Required to include low-tier exclude-rule patterns.' },
163:       excludePatterns: { type: 'array', items: { type: 'string' }, description: 'Candidate exclude patterns for prune-excludes classification.' },
164:       batteryPath: { type: 'string', description: 'Optional gold-query battery path under approved asset roots.' },
165:       includeDetails: { type: 'boolean', description: 'Include per-query battery details in apply response.' },
166:     },
167:     required: [],
168:   },
169: };
170: 
171: 
172: const detectChanges: ToolDefinition = {
173:   name: 'detect_changes',
174:   description: '[L6:Analysis] Read-only preflight: maps a unified-diff input to the structural symbols it touches via line-range overlap against indexed code_nodes. Refuses to answer when graph readiness is anything other than "fresh" — returns status: "blocked" instead of a false-safe empty affectedSymbols[]. Use BEFORE acting on a diff so callers see hard refuse on stale/empty/error state. Token Budget: 1200.',
175:   inputSchema: {
176:     type: 'object', additionalProperties: false,
177:     properties: {
178:       diff: { type: 'string', minLength: 1, description: 'Unified-diff text (e.g. `git diff` output) to map onto indexed symbols' },
179:       rootDir: { type: 'string', description: 'Workspace root (default: process.cwd()). Must resolve under the workspace; symlinks are canonicalized via realpathSync.' },
180:     },
181:     required: ['diff'],
182:   },
183: };
184: 
185: 
186: export const CODE_GRAPH_TOOL_SCHEMAS: ToolDefinition[] = [
187:   codeGraphScan,
188:   codeGraphQuery,
189:   codeGraphStatus,
190:   codeGraphContext,
191:   codeGraphClassifyQueryIntent,
192:   codeGraphVerify,
193:   codeGraphApply,
194:   detectChanges,
195: ];
196: 
197: // Compatibility alias for moved tests and local schema smoke checks.
198: export const TOOL_DEFINITIONS = CODE_GRAPH_TOOL_SCHEMAS;
199: 
200: /**
201:  * Validate raw MCP input against a tool's published inputSchema (BUG-04 fix).
202:  *
203:  * Previously this only checked tool existence and object-shape, so the
204:  * advertised `additionalProperties:false`, `enum`, and `minLength` constraints
205:  * were never enforced and malformed calls reached handlers. We now enforce the
206:  * unambiguous constraints: unknown-key rejection, enum membership, and string
207:  * minLength. Required-field presence is enforced at the dispatcher
208:  * (`getMissingRequiredStringArgs`); numeric range is intentionally left to the
209:  * handler clamps (e.g. `limit`, `maxDepth`) to avoid rejecting values the
210:  * handler safely coerces and bounds. Throws a field-specific Error on the first
211:  * violation so the dispatcher can surface a structured validation error.
212:  */
213: export function validateToolArgs(toolName: string, rawInput: Record<string, unknown>): Record<string, unknown> {
214:   const tool = CODE_GRAPH_TOOL_SCHEMAS.find((definition) => definition.name === toolName);
215:   if (!tool) {
216:     throw new Error(`Unknown tool: ${toolName}`);
217:   }
218:   if (!rawInput || typeof rawInput !== 'object' || Array.isArray(rawInput)) {
219:     throw new Error(`Invalid arguments for ${toolName}: expected object`);
220:   }
221: 
222:   const schema = tool.inputSchema as {
223:     properties?: Record<string, Record<string, unknown>>;
224:     additionalProperties?: boolean;
225:   };
226:   const properties = schema.properties ?? {};
227: 
228:   // additionalProperties:false -> reject keys not in the published schema.
229:   if (schema.additionalProperties === false) {
230:     const allowed = new Set(Object.keys(properties));
231:     const unexpected = Object.keys(rawInput).filter((key) => !allowed.has(key));
232:     if (unexpected.length > 0) {
233:       throw new Error(
234:         `Invalid arguments for ${toolName}: unexpected propert${unexpected.length === 1 ? 'y' : 'ies'}: ${unexpected.join(', ')}`,
235:       );
236:     }
237:   }
238: 
239:   // Validate each PRESENT value: enum membership and string minLength only.
240:   for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(rawInput)) {
241:     const prop = properties[key];
242:     if (!prop || value === undefined || value === null) {
243:       continue;
244:     }
245:     const enumValues = prop.enum as unknown[] | undefined;
246:     if (Array.isArray(enumValues) && !enumValues.includes(value)) {
247:       throw new Error(
248:         `Invalid arguments for ${toolName}: field '${key}' must be one of: ${enumValues.map(String).join(', ')}`,
249:       );
250:     }
251:     const minLength = prop.minLength as number | undefined;
252:     if (typeof minLength === 'number' && typeof value === 'string' && value.length < minLength) {
253:       throw new Error(
254:         `Invalid arguments for ${toolName}: field '${key}' must be at least ${minLength} character(s)`,
255:       );
256:     }
257:   }
258: 
259:   return rawInput;
260: }

(End of file - total 260 lines)
</content>

<content>
1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Code Graph Query Handler
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // MCP tool handler for code_graph_query — queries structural relationships.
5: 
6: import * as graphDb from '../lib/code-graph-db.js';
7: import { ensureCodeGraphReady, type ReadyResult } from '../lib/ensure-ready.js';
8: import type { CodeNode, EdgeType } from '../lib/indexer-types.js';
9: import {
10:   attachGraphEdgeEnrichment,
11:   attachStructuralTrustFields,
12:   type EdgeEvidenceClass,
13:   type HotFileBreadcrumb,
14: } from '../lib/shared/shared-payload.js';
15: import {
16:   buildQueryTrustMetadata,
17:   buildReadinessBlock,
18: } from '../lib/readiness-contract.js';
19: import type {
20:   OutlineQueryNode,
21:   RelationshipQueryEdge,
22: } from '../lib/query-result-adapter.js';
23: // Emit a stable failure-counter metric so operators can distinguish DB /
24: // compute failures from legitimately empty blast radii.
25: import { isSpeckitMetricsEnabled, speckitMetrics } from '../lib/shared/metrics-stub.js';
26: 
27: export interface QueryArgs {
28:   operation: 'outline' | 'calls_from' | 'calls_to' | 'imports_from' | 'imports_to' | 'blast_radius';
29:   subject: string; // filePath, fqName, or symbolId
30:   subjects?: string[];
31:   edgeType?: string;
32:   limit?: number;
33:   includeTransitive?: boolean;
34:   maxDepth?: number;
35:   unionMode?: 'single' | 'multi';
36:   minConfidence?: number;
37: }
38: 
39: const SUPPORTED_EDGE_TYPES = [
40:   'CALLS',
41:   'CONTAINS',
42:   'DECORATES',
43:   'EXPORTS',
44:   'EXTENDS',
45:   'IMPLEMENTS',
46:   'IMPORTS',
47:   'OVERRIDES',
48:   'TESTED_BY',
49:   'TYPE_OF',
50: ] as const satisfies readonly EdgeType[];
51: 
52: const SUPPORTED_EDGE_TYPE_SET = new Set<string>(SUPPORTED_EDGE_TYPES);
53: const AMBIGUOUS_SUBJECT_WARNING_CANDIDATE_LIMIT = 10;
54: const SUPPORTED_RELATIONSHIP_OPERATIONS = [
55:   'calls_from',
56:   'calls_to',
57:   'imports_from',
58:   'imports_to',
59: ] as const;
60: const SUPPORTED_RELATIONSHIP_OPERATION_SET = new Set<string>(SUPPORTED_RELATIONSHIP_OPERATIONS);
61: 
62: /**
63:  * Regex used to decide whether a free-form `subject` argument looks like a
64:  * file path (so we should resolve it through `graphDb.resolveSubjectFilePath`)
65:  * versus a symbol name.
66:  *
67:  * Intentionally broader than `detectLanguage` in `lib/indexer-types.ts`: this
68:  * pattern recognises non-source assets the indexer does not parse but that
69:  * users still pass as subjects (e.g. `README.md`, `package.json`). Keep both
70:  * lists in sync at the language-coverage level only — extension drift between
71:  * the two lists is expected and documented here.
72:  */
73: const FILE_PATH_EXTENSION_PATTERN = /\.(?:[cm]?[jt]sx?|py|bash|zsh|sh|json|md)$/i;
74: const FILE_PATH_SEPARATOR_PATTERN = /[\\/]/;
75: 
76: function isSupportedEdgeType(edgeType: string): edgeType is EdgeType {
77:   return SUPPORTED_EDGE_TYPE_SET.has(edgeType);
78: }
79: 
80: function isSupportedRelationshipOperation(
81:   operation: string,
82: ): operation is (typeof SUPPORTED_RELATIONSHIP_OPERATIONS)[number] {
83:   return SUPPORTED_RELATIONSHIP_OPERATION_SET.has(operation);
84: }
85: 
86: function buildUnknownOperationResponse(operation: string) {
87:   return {
88:     content: [{
89:       type: 'text' as const,
90:       text: JSON.stringify({ status: 'error', error: `Unknown operation: ${operation}` }),
91:     }],
92:   };
93: }
94: 
95: function resolveRequestedEdgeType(args: QueryArgs): { edgeType?: EdgeType; error?: string } {
96:   if (typeof args.edgeType === 'string' && args.edgeType.trim().length > 0) {
97:     const normalizedEdgeType = args.edgeType.trim().toUpperCase();
98:     // Reject typoed filters so empty results never masquerade as valid absence.
99:     if (!isSupportedEdgeType(normalizedEdgeType)) {
100:       return {
101:         error: `Unsupported edgeType "${args.edgeType}". Supported edge types: ${SUPPORTED_EDGE_TYPES.join(', ')}`,
102:       };
103:     }
104:     return { edgeType: normalizedEdgeType };
105:   }
106: 
107:   if (args.operation.startsWith('calls')) {
108:     return { edgeType: 'CALLS' };
109:   }
110: 
111:   if (args.operation.startsWith('imports')) {
112:     return { edgeType: 'IMPORTS' };
113:   }
114: 
115:   return {};
116: }
117: 
118: interface AmbiguousSubjectWarning {
119:   code: 'ambiguous_subject';
120:   subject: string;
121:   matchField: 'fq_name' | 'name' | 'symbol_id';
122:   count: number;
123:   candidates: SubjectCandidateMetadata[];
124:   selectedCandidate: SubjectCandidateMetadata;
125:   selectionReason: string;
126:   message: string;
127: }
128: 
129: interface ResolvedSubject {
130:   symbolId: string | null;
131:   selectedCandidate?: SubjectCandidateMetadata;
132:   warnings?: AmbiguousSubjectWarning[];
133: }
134: 
135: interface SubjectCandidateMetadata {
136:   symbolId: string;
137:   fqName: string | null;
138:   name: string | null;
139:   kind: string | null;
140:   filePath: string | null;
141:   startLine: number | null;
142:   operationEdgeCount?: number;
143:   selectedForOperation?: QueryArgs['operation'];
144: }
145: 
146: interface SubjectMatchResult {
147:   candidates: SubjectCandidateMetadata[];
148:   count: number;
149: }
150: 
151: type BlastRadiusRiskLevel = 'low' | 'medium' | 'high';
152: 
153: interface BlastRadiusDependency {
154:   importedFilePath: string;
155:   importerFilePath: string;
156:   confidence: number;
157: }
158: 
159: interface BlastRadiusAffectedFile {
160:   filePath: string;
161:   depth: number;
162:   hotFileBreadcrumb?: HotFileBreadcrumb;
163: }
164: 
165: interface BlastRadiusFailureFallback {
166:   reason: string;
167:   /**
168:    * Stable machine-readable failure code so operators can distinguish
169:    * failure modes (DB error vs ambiguous subject vs unresolved
170:    * subject vs limit reached vs empty source) without parsing free-form
171:    * `reason` strings. Optional for backward compatibility with older
172:    * call sites.
173:    */
174:   code?: BlastRadiusFailureCode;
175:   partialResult?: {
176:     sourceFiles: string[];
177:     nodes: BlastRadiusAffectedFile[];
178:     affectedFiles: BlastRadiusAffectedFile[];
179:     depthGroups: Record<number, BlastRadiusAffectedFile[]>;
180:   };
181: }
182: 
183: /**
184:  * Stable failure-fallback code values surfaced via
185:  * `BlastRadiusFailureFallback.code`. Adding new values is non-breaking; do
186:  * not rename existing literals once consumers depend on them.
187:  */
188: type BlastRadiusFailureCode =
189:   | 'limit_reached'
190:   | 'unresolved_subject'
191:   | 'ambiguous_subject'
192:   | 'empty_source'
193:   | 'compute_error';
194: 
195: type FallbackDecision = {
196:   nextTool: 'code_graph_scan' | 'code_graph_query' | 'rg';
197:   reason: 'full_scan_required' | 'selective_reindex' | 'scan_failed' | 'scan_declined';
198:   retryAfter?: 'scan_complete';
199: };
200: 
201: function querySubjectMatches(
202:   field: 'fq_name' | 'name',
203:   subject: string,
204: ): SubjectMatchResult {
205:   const d = graphDb.getDb();
206:   const candidates = d.prepare(`
207:     SELECT symbol_id, fq_name, name, kind, file_path, start_line
208:     FROM code_nodes
209:     WHERE ${field} = ?
210:     ORDER BY file_path, start_line, symbol_id
211:   `).all(subject) as Array<{
212:     symbol_id: string;
213:     fq_name: string | null;
214:     name: string | null;
215:     kind: string | null;
216:     file_path: string | null;
217:     start_line: number | null;
218:   }>;
219: 
220:   if (candidates.length === 0) {
221:     return { candidates: [], count: 0 };
222:   }
223: 
224:   return {
225:     candidates: candidates.map((candidate) => ({
226:       symbolId: candidate.symbol_id,
227:       fqName: candidate.fq_name,
228:       name: candidate.name,
229:       kind: candidate.kind,
230:       filePath: candidate.file_path,
231:       startLine: candidate.start_line,
232:     })),
233:     count: candidates.length,
234:   };
235: }
236: 
237: function relationshipEdgeCount(
238:   candidate: SubjectCandidateMetadata,
239:   operation: QueryArgs['operation'],
240:   edgeType: EdgeType | undefined,
241: ): number {
242:   if (!candidate.symbolId) {
243:     return 0;
244:   }
245: 
246:   const resolvedEdgeType = edgeType ?? (
247:     operation.startsWith('calls')
248:       ? 'CALLS'
249:       : operation.startsWith('imports')
250:         ? 'IMPORTS'
251:         : undefined
252:   );
253: 
254:   if (!resolvedEdgeType) {
255:     return 0;
256:   }
257: 
258:   if (operation === 'calls_from' || operation === 'imports_from') {
259:     return graphDb.queryEdgesFrom(candidate.symbolId, resolvedEdgeType).length;
260:   }
261: 
262:   if (operation === 'calls_to' || operation === 'imports_to') {
263:     return graphDb.queryEdgesTo(candidate.symbolId, resolvedEdgeType).length;
264:   }
265: 
266:   return 0;
267: }
268: 
269: function callableKindRank(kind: string | null): number {
270:   switch (kind) {
271:     case 'function':
272:     case 'method':
273:       return 0;
274:     case 'class':
275:       return 1;
276:     case 'variable':
277:       return 2;
278:     default:
279:       return 3;
280:   }
281: }
282: 
283: function pickOperationAwareCandidate(
284:   candidates: SubjectCandidateMetadata[],
285:   operation: QueryArgs['operation'],
286:   edgeType: EdgeType | undefined,
287: ): {
288:   selectedCandidate: SubjectCandidateMetadata;
289:   candidates: SubjectCandidateMetadata[];
290:   selectionReason: string;
291: } {
292:   const rankedCandidates = candidates
293:     .map((candidate) => ({
294:       ...candidate,
295:       operationEdgeCount: relationshipEdgeCount(candidate, operation, edgeType),
296:       selectedForOperation: operation,
297:     }))
298:     .sort((left, right) => (
299:       (right.operationEdgeCount ?? 0) - (left.operationEdgeCount ?? 0)
300:       || callableKindRank(left.kind) - callableKindRank(right.kind)
301:       || (left.filePath ?? '').localeCompare(right.filePath ?? '')
302:       || (left.startLine ?? Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER) - (right.startLine ?? Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER)
303:       || left.symbolId.localeCompare(right.symbolId)
304:     ));
305: 
306:   const selectedCandidate = rankedCandidates[0] ?? candidates[0];
307:   const highestEdgeCount = selectedCandidate?.operationEdgeCount ?? 0;
308:   const hasEdgeSignal = highestEdgeCount > 0;
309:   const selectionReason = hasEdgeSignal
310:     ? `${operation} edge count`
311:     : callableKindRank(selectedCandidate?.kind ?? null) === 0
312:       ? 'callable kind preference'
313:       : 'deterministic file ordering';
314: 
315:   return {
316:     selectedCandidate,
317:     candidates: rankedCandidates,
318:     selectionReason,
319:   };
320: }
321: 
322: function buildAmbiguousSubjectWarning(
323:   subject: string,
324:   matchField: 'fq_name' | 'name' | 'symbol_id',
325:   matchResult: SubjectMatchResult,
326:   selectedCandidate: SubjectCandidateMetadata,
327:   selectionReason: string,
328: ): AmbiguousSubjectWarning {
329:   const truncated = matchResult.count > matchResult.candidates.length;
330:   const shownCount = matchResult.candidates.length;
331:   const countLabel = truncated
332:     ? `${matchResult.count} total; showing first ${shownCount}`
333:     : `${matchResult.count}`;
334: 
335:   return {
336:     code: 'ambiguous_subject',
337:     subject,
338:     matchField,
339:     count: matchResult.count,
340:     candidates: matchResult.candidates,
341:     selectedCandidate,
342:     selectionReason,
343:     message: `Multiple code graph nodes matched subject "${subject}" by ${matchField} (${countLabel}). Selected ${selectedCandidate.symbolId} for ${selectedCandidate.selectedForOperation ?? 'query'} using ${selectionReason}. Use a symbolId to disambiguate the query.`,
344:   };
345: }
346: 
347: /** Resolve a subject string to a symbolId */
348: function resolveSubject(
349:   subject: string,
350:   operation: QueryArgs['operation'],
351:   edgeType: EdgeType | undefined,
352: ): ResolvedSubject {
353:   const d = graphDb.getDb();
354: 
355:   // Try as symbolId first
356:   const byId = d.prepare(`
357:     SELECT symbol_id, fq_name, name, kind, file_path, start_line
358:     FROM code_nodes
359:     WHERE symbol_id = ?
360:   `).get(subject) as {
361:     symbol_id: string;
362:     fq_name: string | null;
363:     name: string | null;
364:     kind: string | null;
365:     file_path: string | null;
366:     start_line: number | null;
367:   } | undefined;
368:   if (byId) {
369:     return {
370:       symbolId: byId.symbol_id,
371:       selectedCandidate: {
372:         symbolId: byId.symbol_id,
373:         fqName: byId.fq_name,
374:         name: byId.name,
375:         kind: byId.kind,
376:         filePath: byId.file_path,
377:         startLine: byId.start_line,
378:         selectedForOperation: operation,
379:       },
380:     };
381:   }
382: 
383:   // Try as fqName
384:   const byFq = querySubjectMatches('fq_name', subject);
385:   if (byFq.count > 0) {
386:     const selection = pickOperationAwareCandidate(byFq.candidates, operation, edgeType);
387:     return {
388:       symbolId: selection.selectedCandidate.symbolId,
389:       selectedCandidate: selection.selectedCandidate,
390:       ...(byFq.count > 1
391:         ? {
392:           warnings: [buildAmbiguousSubjectWarning(subject, 'fq_name', {
393:             ...byFq,
394:             candidates: selection.candidates.slice(0, AMBIGUOUS_SUBJECT_WARNING_CANDIDATE_LIMIT),
395:           }, selection.selectedCandidate, selection.selectionReason)]
396:         }
397:         : {}),
398:     };
399:   }
400: 
401:   // Try as name
402:   const byName = querySubjectMatches('name', subject);
403:   if (byName.count > 0) {
404:     const selection = pickOperationAwareCandidate(byName.candidates, operation, edgeType);
405:     return {
406:       symbolId: selection.selectedCandidate.symbolId,
407:       selectedCandidate: selection.selectedCandidate,
408:       ...(byName.count > 1
409:         ? {
410:           warnings: [buildAmbiguousSubjectWarning(subject, 'name', {
411:             ...byName,
412:             candidates: selection.candidates.slice(0, AMBIGUOUS_SUBJECT_WARNING_CANDIDATE_LIMIT),
413:           }, selection.selectedCandidate, selection.selectionReason)]
414:         }
415:         : {}),
416:     };
417:   }
418: 
419:   return { symbolId: null };
420: }
421: 
422: // Readiness helpers extracted to lib/code-graph/readiness-contract.ts so the
423: // sibling code-graph handlers (scan, status, context) can share one
424: // vocabulary. See that module for durable mapping notes.
425: 
426: function buildGraphQueryPayload<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(
427:   payload: T,
428:   readiness: ReadyResult,
429:   label: string,
430:   graphEdgeEnrichment?: {
431:     edgeEvidenceClass: EdgeEvidenceClass;
432:     numericConfidence: number;
433:   } | null,
434: ) {
435:   const { readiness: _incomingReadiness, ...rest } = payload as Record<string, unknown>;
436:   const { graphMetadata, structuralTrust } = buildQueryTrustMetadata(readiness);
437:   const fallbackDecision = Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(rest, 'fallbackDecision')
438:     ? null
439:     : buildFallbackDecision(readiness);
440:   const payloadWithFallback = fallbackDecision
441:     ? { ...rest, fallbackDecision }
442:     : rest;
443:   const base = graphMetadata
444:     ? { ...payloadWithFallback, graphMetadata, readiness: buildReadinessBlock(readiness) }
445:     : { ...payloadWithFallback, readiness: buildReadinessBlock(readiness) };
446:   const withTrust = attachStructuralTrustFields(
447:     base as T & { readiness: ReturnType<typeof buildReadinessBlock> },
448:     structuralTrust,
449:     { label },
450:   );
451: 
452:   return graphEdgeEnrichment
453:     ? attachGraphEdgeEnrichment(withTrust, graphEdgeEnrichment, {
454:       label: `${label} graph edge enrichment`,
455:     })
456:     : withTrust;
457: }
458: 
459: type OutboundEdgeEntry = ReturnType<typeof graphDb.queryEdgesFrom>[number];
460: type InboundEdgeEntry = ReturnType<typeof graphDb.queryEdgesTo>[number];
461: 
462: interface DanglingEdgeWarning {
463:   code: 'dangling_edge';
464:   operation: QueryArgs['operation'];
465:   missingEndpoint: 'source' | 'target';
466:   count: number;
467:   danglingSymbolIds: string[];
468:   message: string;
469: }
470: 
471: type QueryWarning = AmbiguousSubjectWarning | DanglingEdgeWarning;
472: 
473: function mergeWarnings(...warningSets: Array<QueryWarning[] | undefined>): QueryWarning[] | undefined {
474:   const warnings = warningSets.flatMap((warningSet) => warningSet ?? []);
475:   return warnings.length > 0 ? warnings : undefined;
476: }
477: 
478: function excludeDanglingEdges<TEntry>(
479:   entries: TEntry[],
480:   limit: number,
481:   operation: QueryArgs['operation'],
482:   missingEndpoint: DanglingEdgeWarning['missingEndpoint'],
483:   isResolved: (entry: TEntry) => boolean,
484:   getDanglingId: (entry: TEntry) => string,
485: ): { resolvedEntries: TEntry[]; warnings?: DanglingEdgeWarning[] } {
486:   const resolvedEntries: TEntry[] = [];
487:   const danglingSymbolIds: string[] = [];
488: 
489:   for (const entry of entries) {
490:     if (!isResolved(entry)) {
491:       danglingSymbolIds.push(getDanglingId(entry));
492:       continue;
493:     }
494:     if (resolvedEntries.length < limit) {
495:       resolvedEntries.push(entry);
496:     }
497:   }
498: 
499:   if (danglingSymbolIds.length === 0) {
500:     return { resolvedEntries };
501:   }
502: 
503:   return {
504:     resolvedEntries,
505:     warnings: [{
506:       code: 'dangling_edge',
507:       operation,
508:       missingEndpoint,
509:       count: danglingSymbolIds.length,
510:       danglingSymbolIds: [...new Set(danglingSymbolIds)],
511:       message: `Excluded ${danglingSymbolIds.length} dangling ${operation} edge(s) because the ${missingEndpoint} node could not be resolved. Reindex the code graph to repair the missing node references.`,
512:     }],
513:   };
514: }
515: 
516: /**
517:  * BFS transitive traversal from a symbolId via the given edge type.
518:  *
519:  * Dangling nodes (where queryEdgesFrom/To returns an edge whose endpoint has
520:  * no node row) were previously surfaced as
521:  * successful traversal results with null fqName/filePath. They are now
522:  * flagged as corruption alongside the resolved results so callers can
523:  * trust the absence of `warnings` as "no dangling references".
524:  */
525: interface TransitiveTraversalResult {
526:   nodes: Array<{
527:     symbolId: string;
528:     fqName: string | null;
529:     filePath: string | null;
530:     line: number | null;
531:     depth: number;
532:   }>;
533:   warnings?: DanglingEdgeWarning[];
534: }
535: 
536: function transitiveTraversal(
537:   startId: string,
538:   edgeType: string,
539:   direction: 'from' | 'to',
540:   maxDepth: number,
541:   limit: number,
542:   operation: QueryArgs['operation'],
543: ): TransitiveTraversalResult {
544:   const visited = new Set<string>();
545:   const resultSymbolIds = new Set<string>();
546:   const results: TransitiveTraversalResult['nodes'] = [];
547:   const danglingSymbolIds: string[] = [];
548:   let frontier = [{ id: startId, depth: 0 }];
549:   const missingEndpoint: DanglingEdgeWarning['missingEndpoint'] = direction === 'from' ? 'target' : 'source';
550: 
551:   while (frontier.length > 0 && results.length < limit) {
552:     const next: typeof frontier = [];
553:     for (const item of frontier) {
554:       if (visited.has(item.id) || item.depth >= maxDepth) continue;
555:       visited.add(item.id);
556: 
557:       if (direction === 'from') {
558:         for (const { edge, targetNode } of graphDb.queryEdgesFrom(item.id, edgeType)) {
559:           const nextDepth = item.depth + 1;
560:           if (nextDepth > maxDepth) {
561:             continue;
562:           }
563:           if (targetNode == null) {
564:             danglingSymbolIds.push(edge.targetId);
565:             continue;
566:           }
567:           if (!visited.has(edge.targetId)) {
568:             if (!resultSymbolIds.has(edge.targetId)) {
569:               resultSymbolIds.add(edge.targetId);
570:               results.push({
571:                 symbolId: edge.targetId,
572:                 fqName: targetNode.fqName ?? null,
573:                 filePath: targetNode.filePath ?? null,
574:                 line: targetNode.startLine ?? null,
575:                 depth: nextDepth,
576:               });
577:             }
578:             if (results.length >= limit) break;
579:             next.push({ id: edge.targetId, depth: nextDepth });
580:           }
581:         }
582:       } else {
583:         for (const { edge, sourceNode } of graphDb.queryEdgesTo(item.id, edgeType)) {
584:           const nextDepth = item.depth + 1;
585:           if (nextDepth > maxDepth) {
586:             continue;
587:           }
588:           if (sourceNode == null) {
589:             danglingSymbolIds.push(edge.sourceId);
590:             continue;
591:           }
592:           if (!visited.has(edge.sourceId)) {
593:             if (!resultSymbolIds.has(edge.sourceId)) {
594:               resultSymbolIds.add(edge.sourceId);
595:               results.push({
596:                 symbolId: edge.sourceId,
597:                 fqName: sourceNode.fqName ?? null,
598:                 filePath: sourceNode.filePath ?? null,
599:                 line: sourceNode.startLine ?? null,
600:                 depth: nextDepth,
601:               });
602:             }
603:             if (results.length >= limit) break;
604:             next.push({ id: edge.sourceId, depth: nextDepth });
605:           }
606:         }
607:       }
608: 
609:       if (results.length >= limit) break;
610:     }
611:     frontier = next;
612:   }
613: 
614:   const truncated = results.slice(0, limit);
615:   if (danglingSymbolIds.length === 0) {
616:     return { nodes: truncated };
617:   }
618: 
619:   const uniqueDangling = [...new Set(danglingSymbolIds)];
620:   return {
621:     nodes: truncated,
622:     warnings: [{
623:       code: 'dangling_edge',
624:       operation,
625:       missingEndpoint,
626:       count: uniqueDangling.length,
627:       danglingSymbolIds: uniqueDangling,
628:       message: `Transitive ${operation} traversal encountered ${uniqueDangling.length} dangling ${missingEndpoint} node reference(s). Reindex the code graph to repair the missing node references.`,
629:     }],
630:   };
631: }
632: 
633: function classifyEdgeEvidenceClass(
634:   edgeType: string,
635:   metadata: Record<string, unknown> | undefined,
636: ): EdgeEvidenceClass {
637:   switch (edgeType) {
638:     case 'IMPORTS':
639:     case 'EXPORTS':
640:       return 'import';
641:     case 'EXTENDS':
642:     case 'IMPLEMENTS':
643:     case 'TYPE_OF':
644:       return 'type_reference';
645:     case 'TESTED_BY':
646:       return 'test_coverage';
647:     default:
648:       return metadata?.detectorProvenance === 'heuristic' || metadata?.evidenceClass === 'INFERRED'
649:         ? 'inferred_heuristic'
650:         : 'direct_call';
651:   }
652: }
653: 
654: function clampNumericConfidence(value: unknown): number {
655:   if (typeof value !== 'number' || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
656:     return 0;
657:   }
658:   return Math.max(0, Math.min(1, value));
659: }
660: 
661: function edgeMetadataOutput(edge: OutboundEdgeEntry['edge'] | InboundEdgeEntry['edge']) {
662:   return {
663:     confidence: clampNumericConfidence(edge.metadata?.confidence ?? edge.weight),
664:     numericConfidence: clampNumericConfidence(edge.metadata?.confidence ?? edge.weight),
665:     detectorProvenance: edge.metadata?.detectorProvenance ?? null,
666:     evidenceClass: edge.metadata?.evidenceClass ?? null,
667:     reason: graphDb.sanitizeEdgeMetadataString(edge.metadata?.reason),
668:     step: graphDb.sanitizeEdgeMetadataString(edge.metadata?.step),
669:     edgeEvidenceClass: classifyEdgeEvidenceClass(
670:       edge.edgeType,
671:       edge.metadata as Record<string, unknown> | undefined,
672:     ),
673:   };
674: }
675: 
676: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
677:    Shared relationship-edge mapper
678:    --------------------------------------------------------------
679:    Replaces near-duplicate switch branches for `calls_from`,
680:    `calls_to`, `imports_from`, and `imports_to`. Two inputs control
681:    the variant: (1) direction = outbound (target side) vs inbound
682:    (source side); (2) whether to surface the start line.
683: ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
684: 
685: type RelationshipMappedEdge = RelationshipQueryEdge & {
686:   confidence: number;
687:   numericConfidence: number;
688:   detectorProvenance: string | null;
689:   evidenceClass: string | null;
690:   reason: string | null;
691:   step: string | null;
692:   edgeEvidenceClass: EdgeEvidenceClass;
693: };
694: 
695: function mapOutboundRelationshipEdge(
696:   entry: OutboundEdgeEntry,
697:   options: { includeLine: boolean },
698: ): RelationshipMappedEdge {
699:   return {
700:     target: entry.targetNode?.fqName ?? entry.edge.targetId,
701:     file: entry.targetNode?.filePath,
702:     ...(options.includeLine ? { line: entry.targetNode?.startLine } : {}),
703:     ...edgeMetadataOutput(entry.edge),
704:   };
705: }
706: 
707: function mapInboundRelationshipEdge(
708:   entry: InboundEdgeEntry,
709:   options: { includeLine: boolean },
710: ): RelationshipMappedEdge {
711:   return {
712:     source: entry.sourceNode?.fqName ?? entry.edge.sourceId,
713:     file: entry.sourceNode?.filePath,
714:     ...(options.includeLine ? { line: entry.sourceNode?.startLine } : {}),
715:     ...edgeMetadataOutput(entry.edge),
716:   };
717: }
718: 
719: function extractOutboundFilePaths(entries: OutboundEdgeEntry[]): string[] {
720:   return entries
721:     .map((entry) => entry.targetNode?.filePath)
722:     .filter((value): value is string => typeof value === 'string');
723: }
724: 
725: function extractInboundFilePaths(entries: InboundEdgeEntry[]): string[] {
726:   return entries
727:     .map((entry) => entry.sourceNode?.filePath)
728:     .filter((value): value is string => typeof value === 'string');
729: }
730: 
731: function subjectLooksLikeFilePath(subject: string): boolean {
732:   return FILE_PATH_SEPARATOR_PATTERN.test(subject) || FILE_PATH_EXTENSION_PATTERN.test(subject);
733: }
734: 
735: function resolveRelationshipFilePath(subject: string): string | null {
736:   if (!subjectLooksLikeFilePath(subject)) {
737:     return null;
738:   }
739: 
740:   const resolvedFilePath = graphDb.resolveSubjectFilePath(subject);
741:   return typeof resolvedFilePath === 'string' && resolvedFilePath.length > 0
742:     ? resolvedFilePath
743:     : null;
744: }
745: 
746: function queryRelationshipFileSymbols(filePath: string): CodeNode[] {
747:   return graphDb
748:     .queryOutline(filePath)
749:     .filter((node) => typeof node.symbolId === 'string' && node.symbolId.length > 0);
750: }
751: 
752: function relationshipFilePayload(input: {
753:   operation: QueryArgs['operation'];
754:   filePath: string;
755:   symbols: CodeNode[];
756:   edgeType: EdgeType | undefined;
757:   limit: number;
758: }): {
759:   result: {
760:     operation: QueryArgs['operation'];
761:     filePath: string;
762:     subjectKind: 'file_path';
763:     symbolCount: number;
764:     edgeType: EdgeType | undefined;
765:     edges: RelationshipMappedEdge[];
766:     warnings?: DanglingEdgeWarning[];
767:     hotFileBreadcrumbs: ReturnType<typeof buildHotFileBreadcrumbs>;
768:   };
769:   edgeEnrichment: ReturnType<typeof summarizeWeakestGraphEdgeEnrichment>;
770: } {
771:   const sourceIds = input.symbols.map((node) => node.symbolId);
772:   if (input.operation === 'calls_from' || input.operation === 'imports_from') {
773:     const entries = sourceIds.flatMap((symbolId) => graphDb.queryEdgesFrom(symbolId, input.edgeType));
774:     const { resolvedEntries, warnings } = excludeDanglingEdges(
775:       entries,
776:       input.limit,
777:       input.operation,
778:       'target',
779:       (entry: OutboundEdgeEntry) => entry.targetNode != null,
780:       (entry: OutboundEdgeEntry) => entry.edge.targetId,
781:     );
782:     const includeLine = input.operation === 'calls_from';
783:     const edges = resolvedEntries.map((entry) => mapOutboundRelationshipEdge(entry, { includeLine }));
784:     return {
785:       result: {
786:         operation: input.operation,
787:         filePath: input.filePath,
788:         subjectKind: 'file_path',
789:         symbolCount: input.symbols.length,
790:         edgeType: input.edgeType,
791:         edges,
792:         ...(warnings ? { warnings } : {}),
793:         hotFileBreadcrumbs: buildHotFileBreadcrumbs(extractOutboundFilePaths(resolvedEntries)),
794:       },
795:       edgeEnrichment: summarizeWeakestGraphEdgeEnrichment(edges),
796:     };
797:   }
798: 
799:   const entries = sourceIds.flatMap((symbolId) => graphDb.queryEdgesTo(symbolId, input.edgeType));
800:   const { resolvedEntries, warnings } = excludeDanglingEdges(
801:     entries,
802:     input.limit,
803:     input.operation,
804:     'source',
805:     (entry: InboundEdgeEntry) => entry.sourceNode != null,
806:     (entry: InboundEdgeEntry) => entry.edge.sourceId,
807:   );
808:   const includeLine = input.operation === 'calls_to';
809:   const edges = resolvedEntries.map((entry) => mapInboundRelationshipEdge(entry, { includeLine }));
810:   return {
811:     result: {
812:       operation: input.operation,
813:       filePath: input.filePath,
814:       subjectKind: 'file_path',
815:       symbolCount: input.symbols.length,
816:       edgeType: input.edgeType,
817:       edges,
818:       ...(warnings ? { warnings } : {}),
819:       hotFileBreadcrumbs: buildHotFileBreadcrumbs(extractInboundFilePaths(resolvedEntries)),
820:     },
821:     edgeEnrichment: summarizeWeakestGraphEdgeEnrichment(edges),
822:   };
823: }
824: 
825: const EDGE_EVIDENCE_CLASS_WEAKNESS: Record<EdgeEvidenceClass, number> = {
826:   inferred_heuristic: 0,
827:   test_coverage: 1,
828:   type_reference: 2,
829:   import: 3,
830:   direct_call: 4,
831: };
832: 
833: function summarizeWeakestGraphEdgeEnrichment(
834:   edges: Array<{
835:     edgeEvidenceClass: EdgeEvidenceClass;
836:     numericConfidence: number;
837:   }>,
838: ): {
839:   edgeEvidenceClass: EdgeEvidenceClass;
840:   numericConfidence: number;
841: } | null {
842:   let weakest: {
843:     edgeEvidenceClass: EdgeEvidenceClass;
844:     numericConfidence: number;
845:   } | null = null;
846: 
847:   for (const edge of edges) {
848:     if (weakest == null || edge.numericConfidence < weakest.numericConfidence) {
849:       weakest = edge;
850:       continue;
851:     }
852: 
853:     if (
854:       edge.numericConfidence === weakest.numericConfidence
855:       && EDGE_EVIDENCE_CLASS_WEAKNESS[edge.edgeEvidenceClass]
856:       < EDGE_EVIDENCE_CLASS_WEAKNESS[weakest.edgeEvidenceClass]
857:     ) {
858:       weakest = edge;
859:     }
860:   }
861: 
862:   return weakest;
863: }
864: 
865: function shouldBlockReadPath(readiness: ReadyResult): boolean {
866:   // False-safe contract (BUG-01 fix): answer ONLY on a fresh graph. Any
867:   // non-fresh state must block rather than return a possibly-wrong structural
868:   // answer with status:'ok'. The previous predicate keyed only on
869:   // `action === 'full_scan' && !inlineIndexPerformed`, which let two non-fresh
870:   // states slip through: (a) a deleted-files-only `freshness:'stale', action:'none'`
871:   // result, and (b) a FAILED inline selective_reindex (`freshness:'stale',
872:   // inlineIndexPerformed:false`). Both returned ok over a stale graph. We now
873:   // gate on freshness directly and also refuse a failed gold-verification gate,
874:   // matching `detect_changes` readinessRequiresBlock for a single contract.
875:   return readiness.freshness !== 'fresh' || readiness.verificationGate === 'fail';
876: }
877: 
878: function buildFallbackDecision(readiness: ReadyResult): FallbackDecision | null {
879:   if (readiness.freshness === 'error') {
880:     return {
881:       nextTool: 'rg',
882:       reason: 'scan_failed',
883:     };
884:   }
885: 
886:   // Any non-fresh graph that reached the block path needs a scan before reads
887:   // can be trusted (full_scan, empty, or a stale graph a selective reindex
888:   // could not heal).
889:   if (readiness.freshness !== 'fresh') {
890:     return {
891:       nextTool: 'code_graph_scan',
892:       reason: readiness.action === 'selective_reindex' ? 'selective_reindex' : 'full_scan_required',
893:       retryAfter: 'scan_complete',
894:     };
895:   }
896: 
897:   return null;
898: }
899: 
900: function buildBlockedReadPayload(
901:   readiness: ReadyResult,
902:   operation: QueryArgs['operation'],
903:   subject: string,
904: ) {
905:   const fallbackDecision = buildFallbackDecision(readiness);
906:   // Surface scope + manifest diagnostics directly on the
907:   // blocked payload's `data` object so operators can route on
908:   // them without parsing `data.readiness`. Backward compatible:
909:   // legacy callers reading `status` + `data.blockReason` continue
910:   // to work; nested `data.readiness` still carries the same fields.
911:   return {
912:     status: 'blocked',
913:     message: `code_graph_full_scan_required: ${readiness.reason}`,
914:     data: buildGraphQueryPayload({
915:       operation,
916:       subject,
917:       blocked: true,
918:       degraded: true,
919:       graphAnswersOmitted: true,
920:       requiredAction: 'code_graph_scan',
921:       blockReason: 'full_scan_required',
922:       reason: readiness.reason,
923:       activeScope: readiness.activeScope ?? null,
924:       storedScope: readiness.storedScope ?? null,
925:       manifestCount: readiness.manifestCount ?? null,
926:       manifestDigest: readiness.manifestDigest ?? null,
927:       ...(fallbackDecision ? { fallbackDecision } : {}),
928:     }, readiness, `code_graph_query ${operation} blocked payload`),
929:   };
930: }
931: 
932: function buildHotFileBreadcrumbs(filePaths: string[]): Array<{
933:   filePath: string;
934:   hotFileBreadcrumb: HotFileBreadcrumb;
935: }> {
936:   const uniqueFilePaths = [...new Set(filePaths)];
937:   if (uniqueFilePaths.length === 0) {
938:     return [];
939:   }
940: 
941:   const degreeRows = graphDb.queryFileDegrees(uniqueFilePaths);
942:   const positiveDegrees = degreeRows
943:     .map((row) => row.degree)
944:     .filter((degree) => degree > 0)
945:     .sort((left, right) => right - left);
946:   if (positiveDegrees.length === 0) {
947:     return [];
948:   }
949: 
950:   const topCount = Math.max(1, Math.ceil(positiveDegrees.length * 0.1));
951:   const threshold = Math.min(20, positiveDegrees[topCount - 1] ?? positiveDegrees[0]);
952: 
953:   return degreeRows
954:     .filter((row) => row.degree >= threshold)
955:     .map((row) => ({
956:       filePath: row.filePath,
957:       hotFileBreadcrumb: {
958:         degree: row.degree,
959:         changeCarefullyReason: `High-degree node; change carefully because changes here ripple to ${row.degree} dependents.`,
960:       },
961:     }));
962: }
963: 
964: function parseEdgeMetadataConfidence(metadataJson: unknown, weight: unknown): number {
965:   if (typeof metadataJson === 'string' && metadataJson.length > 0) {
966:     try {
967:       const metadata = JSON.parse(metadataJson) as Record<string, unknown>;
968:       return clampNumericConfidence(metadata.confidence ?? weight);
969:     } catch {
970:       return clampNumericConfidence(weight);
971:     }
972:   }
973:   return clampNumericConfidence(weight);
974: }
975: 
976: function queryImportDependentsForBlastRadius(minConfidence: number): BlastRadiusDependency[] {
977:   if (minConfidence <= 0) {
978:     return graphDb.queryFileImportDependents().map((edge) => ({
979:       ...edge,
980:       confidence: 1,
981:     }));
982:   }
983: 
984:   const d = graphDb.getDb();
985:   const rows = d.prepare(`
986:     SELECT
987:       target.file_path AS imported_file_path,
988:       source.file_path AS importer_file_path,
989:       edge.weight AS weight,
990:       edge.metadata AS metadata
991:     FROM code_edges edge
992:     INNER JOIN code_nodes source ON source.symbol_id = edge.source_id
993:     INNER JOIN code_nodes target ON target.symbol_id = edge.target_id
994:     WHERE UPPER(edge.edge_type) = 'IMPORTS'
995:       AND source.file_path != target.file_path
996:   `).all() as Array<{
997:     imported_file_path: string;
998:     importer_file_path: string;
999:     weight: number | null;
1000:     metadata: string | null;
1001:   }>;
1002: 
1003:   return rows
1004:     .map((row) => ({
1005:       importedFilePath: row.imported_file_path,
1006:       importerFilePath: row.importer_file_path,
1007:       confidence: parseEdgeMetadataConfidence(row.metadata, row.weight ?? 1),
1008:     }))
1009:     .filter((edge) => edge.confidence >= minConfidence);
1010: }
1011: 
1012: function buildDepthGroups(
1013:   affectedFiles: BlastRadiusAffectedFile[],
1014:   maxDepth: number,
1015: ): Record<number, BlastRadiusAffectedFile[]> {
1016:   const depthGroups: Record<number, BlastRadiusAffectedFile[]> = {};
1017:   for (let depth = 1; depth <= Math.max(0, maxDepth); depth++) {
1018:     depthGroups[depth] = [];
1019:   }
1020:   for (const affectedFile of affectedFiles) {
1021:     depthGroups[affectedFile.depth] ??= [];
1022:     depthGroups[affectedFile.depth].push(affectedFile);
1023:   }
1024:   return depthGroups;
1025: }
1026: 
1027: /**
1028:  * Classify a blast-radius response by depth-one fanout.
1029:  *
1030:  * Thresholds (008/D9 — documented):
1031:  *   - **high**: any of (a) ambiguityCandidates non-empty, (b) depth-one fanout > 10
1032:  *   - **medium**: depth-one fanout in [4, 10]
1033:  *   - **low**: depth-one fanout in [0, 3]
1034:  *
1035:  * Depth-one is used (not max depth) because the immediate consumers of
1036:  * the seed are the strongest blast-radius signal in practice.
1037:  */
1038: function classifyBlastRadiusRisk(
1039:   depthGroups: Record<number, BlastRadiusAffectedFile[]>,
1040:   ambiguityCandidates: SubjectCandidateMetadata[],
1041: ): BlastRadiusRiskLevel {
1042:   const depthOneCount = depthGroups[1]?.length ?? 0;
1043:   if (ambiguityCandidates.length > 0 || depthOneCount > 10) {
1044:     return 'high';
1045:   }
1046:   if (depthOneCount >= 4) {
1047:     return 'medium';
1048:   }
1049:   return 'low';
1050: }
1051: 
1052: function buildPartialBlastRadiusResult(
1053:   sourceFiles: string[],
1054:   nodes: BlastRadiusAffectedFile[],
1055:   affectedFiles: BlastRadiusAffectedFile[],
1056:   depthGroups: Record<number, BlastRadiusAffectedFile[]>,
1057: ): NonNullable<BlastRadiusFailureFallback['partialResult']> {
1058:   return {
1059:     sourceFiles,
1060:     nodes,
1061:     affectedFiles,
1062:     depthGroups,
1063:   };
1064: }
1065: 
1066: function computeBlastRadius(
1067:   sourceFiles: string[],
1068:   maxDepth: number,
1069:   limit: number,
1070:   minConfidence = 0,
1071:   ambiguityCandidates: SubjectCandidateMetadata[] = [],
1072: ) {
1073:   const importedBy = new Map<string, Set<string>>();
1074:   const importEdges = queryImportDependentsForBlastRadius(minConfidence);
1075:   for (const edge of importEdges) {
1076:     const importers = importedBy.get(edge.importedFilePath) ?? new Set<string>();
1077:     importers.add(edge.importerFilePath);
1078:     importedBy.set(edge.importedFilePath, importers);
1079:   }
1080: 
1081:   const affectedByFile = new Map<string, number>();
1082:   const normalizedSources = [...new Set(sourceFiles)];
1083: 
1084:   // Tracks whether the BFS frontier still held unexpanded, never-visited
1085:   // neighbors when it hit `maxDepth`. Without this, a caller asking "what
1086:   // depends on X" cannot distinguish a fully-traversed graph from one cut off
1087:   // at the depth bound: `overflowed` only reports the count-based slice, not
1088:   // the depth horizon. We flip this only when a neighbor was dropped *solely*
1089:   // because it lay beyond `maxDepth` (not because it was already visited at a
1090:   // shallower depth, where no reachability information is lost).
1091:   let depthTruncated = false;
1092: 
1093:   for (const sourceFile of normalizedSources) {
1094:     const visited = new Set<string>([sourceFile]);
1095:     const queue: Array<{ filePath: string; depth: number }> = [{ filePath: sourceFile, depth: 0 }];
1096: 
1097:     while (queue.length > 0) {
1098:       const current = queue.shift();
1099:       if (!current) break;
1100: 
1101:       const importers = importedBy.get(current.filePath);
1102:       if (!importers) continue;
1103: 
1104:       for (const importerFilePath of importers) {
1105:         const nextDepth = current.depth + 1;
1106:         if (visited.has(importerFilePath)) {
1107:           continue;
1108:         }
1109:         if (nextDepth > maxDepth) {
1110:           // A reachable, never-visited dependent exists beyond the depth bound,
1111:           // so the reported set is depth-incomplete.
1112:           depthTruncated = true;
1113:           continue;
1114:         }
1115:         visited.add(importerFilePath);
1116:         queue.push({ filePath: importerFilePath, depth: nextDepth });
1117: 
1118:         if (normalizedSources.includes(importerFilePath)) {
1119:           continue;
1120:         }
1121: 
1122:         const previousDepth = affectedByFile.get(importerFilePath);
1123:         if (previousDepth === undefined || nextDepth < previousDepth) {
1124:           affectedByFile.set(importerFilePath, nextDepth);
1125:         }
1126:       }
1127:     }
1128:   }
1129: 
1130:   // Detect true overflow by comparing the full traversal size against `limit`
1131:   // BEFORE slicing. Previously `affectedFiles.length >= limit`
1132:   // false-positived whenever the reachable set happened to equal `limit`
1133:   // exactly. We over-collect (`limit + 1` semantically — here the whole
1134:   // BFS frontier is already in `affectedByFile`) and slice down only after
1135:   // recording overflow.
1136:   const totalAffectedBeforeSlice = affectedByFile.size;
1137:   let affectedFiles = [...affectedByFile.entries()]
1138:     .map(([filePath, depth]) => ({ filePath, depth }))
1139:     .sort((left, right) => left.depth - right.depth || left.filePath.localeCompare(right.filePath))
1140:     .slice(0, limit);
1141:   const overflowed = totalAffectedBeforeSlice > limit;
1142:   const breadcrumbByFile = new Map(
1143:     buildHotFileBreadcrumbs([
1144:       ...normalizedSources,
1145:       ...affectedFiles.map((entry) => entry.filePath),
1146:     ]).map((entry) => [entry.filePath, entry.hotFileBreadcrumb]),
1147:   );
1148:   affectedFiles = affectedFiles.map((entry) => ({
1149:     ...entry,
1150:     ...(breadcrumbByFile.get(entry.filePath) ? { hotFileBreadcrumb: breadcrumbByFile.get(entry.filePath) } : {}),
1151:   }));
1152: 
1153:   const seedNodes = normalizedSources.map((filePath) => ({
1154:     filePath,
1155:     depth: 0,
1156:     isSeed: true,
1157:     ...(breadcrumbByFile.get(filePath) ? { hotFileBreadcrumb: breadcrumbByFile.get(filePath) } : {}),
1158:   }));
1159:   const depthGroups = buildDepthGroups(affectedFiles, maxDepth);
1160: 
1161:   return {
1162:     sourceFiles: normalizedSources,
1163:     nodes: [
1164:       ...seedNodes,
1165:       ...affectedFiles,
1166:     ],
1167:     affectedFiles,
1168:     depthGroups,
1169:     riskLevel: classifyBlastRadiusRisk(depthGroups, ambiguityCandidates),
1170:     minConfidence,
1171:     ambiguityCandidates,
1172:     hotFileBreadcrumbs: [...breadcrumbByFile.entries()].map(([filePath, hotFileBreadcrumb]) => ({
1173:       filePath,
1174:       hotFileBreadcrumb,
1175:     })),
1176:     // Omitted when the traversal reached the full depth horizon, mirroring the
1177:     // `overflowed`-driven conditional spread below so the field is absent
1178:     // (rather than `false`) in the common, complete case.
1179:     ...(depthTruncated ? { depthTruncated: true } : {}),
1180:     ...(overflowed
1181:       ? {
1182:         failureFallback: {
1183:           reason: 'limit_reached',
1184:           code: 'limit_reached',
1185:           partialResult: buildPartialBlastRadiusResult(
1186:             normalizedSources,
1187:             [...seedNodes, ...affectedFiles],
1188:             affectedFiles,
1189:             depthGroups,
1190:           ),
1191:         } satisfies BlastRadiusFailureFallback,
1192:       }
1193:       : {}),
1194:   };
1195: }
1196: 
1197: /** Handle code_graph_query tool call */
1198: export async function handleCodeGraphQuery(args: QueryArgs): Promise<{ content: Array<{ type: string; text: string }> }> {
1199:   let readiness: ReadyResult = {
1200:     freshness: 'empty' as const,
1201:     action: 'none' as const,
1202:     inlineIndexPerformed: false,
1203:     reason: 'readiness check not run',
1204:   };
1205: 
1206:   // Auto-trigger: ensure graph is fresh before querying
1207:   try {
1208:     readiness = await ensureCodeGraphReady(process.cwd(), {
1209:       allowInlineIndex: true,
1210:       allowInlineFullScan: false,
1211:       allowGuardedInlineFullScan: true,
1212:     });
1213:   } catch (error) {
1214:     // Emit 'unavailable' trust-state on readiness-crash so query consumers
1215:     // see the same canonical vocabulary
1216:     // as code_graph_context. Previously this path dropped the readiness
1217:     // block entirely and only returned a status:'error' string.
1218:     const reason = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
1219:     const crashReadiness: ReadyResult = {
1220:       freshness: 'error' as const,
1221:       action: 'none' as const,
1222:       inlineIndexPerformed: false,
1223:       reason: 'readiness_check_crashed',
1224:     };
1225:     const crashBlock = buildReadinessBlock(crashReadiness);
1226:     return {
1227:       content: [{
1228:         type: 'text',
1229:         text: JSON.stringify({
1230:           // BUG-07: align with code_graph_context + detect_changes — a non-fresh
1231:           // / unavailable graph (here: readiness probe crashed) refuses with
1232:           // status:'blocked', not 'error', so all three read handlers share one
1233:           // refusal token. The structured readiness block + rg fallback are
1234:           // preserved so consumers still see the crash detail.
1235:           status: 'blocked',
1236:           message: `code_graph_not_ready: ${reason}`,
1237:           data: {
1238:             readiness: crashBlock,
1239:             canonicalReadiness: crashBlock.canonicalReadiness,
1240:             trustState: crashBlock.trustState,
1241:             fallbackDecision: buildFallbackDecision(crashReadiness),
1242:           },
1243:         }),
1244:       }],
1245:     };
1246:   }
1247: 
1248:   const { operation, subject } = args;
1249:   const limit = Math.max(1, Math.min(Number(args.limit) || 50, 1000));
1250:   const { edgeType: requestedEdgeType, error: edgeTypeError } = resolveRequestedEdgeType(args);
1251:   const rawMaxDepth = args.maxDepth === undefined ? 3 : Number(args.maxDepth);
1252:   const maxDepth = Math.max(0, Math.min(Number.isFinite(rawMaxDepth) ? rawMaxDepth : 3, 20));
1253: 
1254:   if (edgeTypeError) {
1255:     return {
1256:       content: [{
1257:         type: 'text',
1258:         text: JSON.stringify({ status: 'error', error: edgeTypeError }),
1259:       }],
1260:     };
1261:   }
1262: 
1263:   if (shouldBlockReadPath(readiness)) {
1264:     return {
1265:       content: [{
1266:         type: 'text',
1267:         text: JSON.stringify(buildBlockedReadPayload(readiness, operation, subject), null, 2),
1268:       }],
1269:     };
1270:   }
1271: 
1272:   if (operation === 'outline') {
1273:     const resolvedSubject = graphDb.resolveSubjectFilePath(subject);
1274:     if (typeof resolvedSubject !== 'string' || resolvedSubject.length === 0) {
1275:       return {
1276:         content: [{
1277:           type: 'text',
1278:           text: JSON.stringify({
1279:             status: 'error',
1280:             error: `Could not resolve outline subject: ${subject}`,
1281:           }),
1282:         }],
1283:       };
1284:     }
1285: 
1286:     const nodes = graphDb.queryOutline(resolvedSubject);
1287:     const limited = nodes.slice(0, limit);
1288:     const outlineNodes: OutlineQueryNode[] = limited.map(n => ({
1289:       name: n.name,
1290:       kind: n.kind,
1291:       fqName: n.fqName,
1292:       line: n.startLine,
1293:       signature: n.signature,
1294:       symbolId: n.symbolId,
1295:     }));
1296:     return {
1297:       content: [{
1298:         type: 'text',
1299:         text: JSON.stringify({
1300:           status: 'ok',
1301:           data: buildGraphQueryPayload({
1302:             operation: 'outline',
1303:             filePath: resolvedSubject,
1304:             nodeCount: outlineNodes.length,
1305:             nodes: outlineNodes,
1306:           }, readiness, 'code_graph_query outline payload'),
1307:         }, null, 2),
1308:       }],
1309:     };
1310:   }
1311: 
1312:   if (operation === 'blast_radius') {
1313:     // Honor the documented `includeTransitive` flag for blast_radius: the schema
1314:     // describes it as "Enable multi-hop BFS traversal" (default false) with
1315:     // `maxDepth` applying only when it is true. Default returns direct importers
1316:     // only (depth 1); the flag opts into multi-hop closure up to maxDepth. (The
1317:     // call-graph operations gate on the same flag in their own branch.)
1318:     const effectiveDepth = args.includeTransitive ? maxDepth : 1;
1319:     const rawSubjects = args.unionMode === 'multi'
1320:       ? [subject, ...(args.subjects ?? [])]
1321:       : [subject];
1322:     const sourceFiles: string[] = [];
1323:     const ambiguityCandidates: SubjectCandidateMetadata[] = [];
1324:     const minConfidence = clampNumericConfidence(args.minConfidence ?? 0);
1325: 
1326:     for (const candidate of rawSubjects) {
1327:       const candidateLooksLikeFilePath = subjectLooksLikeFilePath(candidate);
1328:       let resolvedPathCandidate: string | null = null;
1329:       if (candidateLooksLikeFilePath) {
1330:         const resolvedFileCandidate = graphDb.resolveSubjectFilePath(candidate);
1331:         if (typeof resolvedFileCandidate === 'string' && resolvedFileCandidate.length > 0) {
1332:           sourceFiles.push(resolvedFileCandidate);
1333:           continue;
1334:         }
1335:         resolvedPathCandidate = resolvedFileCandidate;
1336:       }
1337: 
1338:       const byFqName = querySubjectMatches('fq_name', candidate);
1339:       if (byFqName.count > 1) {
1340:         ambiguityCandidates.push(...byFqName.candidates.slice(0, AMBIGUOUS_SUBJECT_WARNING_CANDIDATE_LIMIT));
1341:         continue;
1342:       }
1343:       if (byFqName.count === 0) {
1344:         const byName = querySubjectMatches('name', candidate);
1345:         if (byName.count > 1) {
1346:           ambiguityCandidates.push(...byName.candidates.slice(0, AMBIGUOUS_SUBJECT_WARNING_CANDIDATE_LIMIT));
1347:           continue;
1348:         }
1349:       }
1350: 
1351:       const resolvedSubject = resolvedPathCandidate ?? graphDb.resolveSubjectFilePath(candidate);
1352:       if (typeof resolvedSubject !== 'string' || resolvedSubject.length === 0) {
1353:         // Preserve any sibling seeds we have already resolved ahead of this
1354:         // failed candidate. Returning `nodes: []` here would
1355:         // discard work the caller can still use; instead surface the
1356:         // already-resolved seeds so multi-subject blast-radius queries
1357:         // degrade gracefully when one subject fails to resolve.
1358:         const preservedDepthGroups = buildDepthGroups([], effectiveDepth);
1359:         const preservedSeedNodes = sourceFiles.map((filePath) => ({
1360:           filePath,
1361:           depth: 0,
1362:           isSeed: true,
1363:         }));
1364:         return {
1365:           content: [{
1366:             type: 'text',
1367:             text: JSON.stringify({
1368:               status: 'ok',
1369:               data: buildGraphQueryPayload({
1370:                 operation,
1371:                 sourceFiles,
1372:                 nodes: preservedSeedNodes,
1373:                 affectedFiles: [],
1374:                 depthGroups: preservedDepthGroups,
1375:                 riskLevel: classifyBlastRadiusRisk(preservedDepthGroups, []),
1376:                 minConfidence,
1377:                 ambiguityCandidates: [],
1378:                 failureFallback: {
1379:                   reason: `unresolved_subject: ${candidate}`,
1380:                   code: 'unresolved_subject',
1381:                   partialResult: buildPartialBlastRadiusResult(
1382:                     sourceFiles,
1383:                     preservedSeedNodes,
1384:                     [],
1385:                     preservedDepthGroups,
1386:                   ),
1387:                 } satisfies BlastRadiusFailureFallback,
1388:               }, readiness, 'code_graph_query blast_radius fallback payload'),
1389:             }, null, 2),
1390:           }],
1391:         };
1392:       }
1393:       sourceFiles.push(resolvedSubject);
1394:     }
1395: 
1396:     if (ambiguityCandidates.length > 0) {
1397:       const depthGroups = buildDepthGroups([], effectiveDepth);
1398:       return {
1399:         content: [{
1400:           type: 'text',
1401:           text: JSON.stringify({
1402:             status: 'ok',
1403:             data: buildGraphQueryPayload({
1404:               operation,
1405:               sourceFiles,
1406:               nodes: [],
1407:               affectedFiles: [],
1408:               depthGroups,
1409:               riskLevel: classifyBlastRadiusRisk(depthGroups, ambiguityCandidates),
1410:               minConfidence,
1411:               ambiguityCandidates,
1412:               failureFallback: {
1413:                 reason: 'ambiguous_subject',
1414:                 code: 'ambiguous_subject',
1415:                 partialResult: buildPartialBlastRadiusResult(sourceFiles, [], [], depthGroups),
1416:               } satisfies BlastRadiusFailureFallback,
1417:             }, readiness, 'code_graph_query blast_radius ambiguity payload'),
1418:           }, null, 2),
1419:         }],
1420:       };
1421:     }
1422: 
1423:     if (sourceFiles.length === 0) {
1424:       const depthGroups = buildDepthGroups([], effectiveDepth);
1425:       return {
1426:         content: [{
1427:           type: 'text',
1428:           text: JSON.stringify({
1429:             status: 'ok',
1430:             data: buildGraphQueryPayload({
1431:               operation,
1432:               sourceFiles,
1433:               nodes: [],
1434:               affectedFiles: [],
1435:               depthGroups,
1436:               riskLevel: classifyBlastRadiusRisk(depthGroups, []),
1437:               minConfidence,
1438:               ambiguityCandidates: [],
1439:               failureFallback: {
1440:                 reason: `Could not resolve blast-radius sources: ${rawSubjects.join(', ')}`,
1441:                 code: 'empty_source',
1442:                 partialResult: buildPartialBlastRadiusResult(sourceFiles, [], [], depthGroups),
1443:               } satisfies BlastRadiusFailureFallback,
1444:             }, readiness, 'code_graph_query blast_radius empty-source payload'),
1445:           }, null, 2),
1446:         }],
1447:       };
1448:     }
1449: 
1450:     let blastRadius;
1451:     try {
1452:       blastRadius = graphDb.getDb().transaction(() => computeBlastRadius(sourceFiles, effectiveDepth, limit, minConfidence, ambiguityCandidates))();
1453:     } catch (error) {
1454:       const depthGroups = buildDepthGroups([], effectiveDepth);
1455:       const reason = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
1456:       // Surface a stable `code` and emit a warning log + metric so operators
1457:       // can distinguish a genuine compute / DB failure from a
1458:       // legitimately empty blast radius (which has no failureFallback).
1459:       console.warn(
1460:         `[code-graph-query] blast_radius compute failure: ${reason} (sources=${sourceFiles.length})`,
1461:       );
1462:       try {
1463:         if (isSpeckitMetricsEnabled()) {
1464:           speckitMetrics.incrementCounter(
