1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Code Graph Context Handler
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // MCP tool handler for code_graph_context — LLM-oriented graph neighborhoods.
5: 
6: import { buildContext, type ContextArgs, type QueryMode } from '../lib/code-graph-context.js';
7: import type { CodeGraphSeed } from '../lib/seed-resolver.js';
8: import { ensureCodeGraphReady, type ReadyResult } from '../lib/ensure-ready.js';
9: import * as graphDb from '../lib/code-graph-db.js';
10: import { buildReadinessBlock } from '../lib/readiness-contract.js';
11: 
12: export interface ContextHandlerArgs {
13:   input?: string;
14:   queryMode?: string;
15:   subject?: string;
16:   seeds?: Array<{
17:     filePath?: string;
18:     file_path?: string;
19:     startLine?: number;
20:     start_line?: number;
21:     endLine?: number;
22:     end_line?: number;
23:     query?: string;
24:     provider?: string;
25:     source?: string;
26:     file?: string;
27:     range?: { start: number; end: number };
28:     lines?: { start?: number; end?: number };
29:     score?: number;
30:     snippet?: string;
31:     content?: string;
32:     symbolName?: string;
33:     kind?: string;
34:     nodeId?: string;
35:     symbolId?: string;
36:   }>;
37:   budgetTokens?: number;
38:   profile?: string;
39:   includeTrace?: boolean;
40: }
41: 
42: interface NormalizedSeedSource {
43:   source?: string;
44:   filePath: string;
45:   startLine?: number;
46:   endLine?: number;
47:   symbolId?: string;
48:   symbolName?: string;
49: }
50: 
51: type ContextReadiness = ReadyResult & { error?: string };
52: 
53: interface ContextFallbackDecision {
54:   nextTool: 'rg' | 'code_graph_scan';
55:   reason: string;
56: }
57: 
58: function shouldBlockReadPath(readiness: ReadyResult): boolean {
59:   // A crashed readiness probe (`freshness: 'error'`) MUST also block before
60:   // `buildContext()` so the degraded envelope preserves canonical
61:   // readiness/trustState and emits an `rg` recovery signal — the same shape
62:   // that `code_graph_query` already ships via `fallbackDecision`.
63:   // Falling through to `buildContext()` on a crashed probe loses the
64:   // structured envelope and downgrades the response to a generic 'error'.
65:   if (readiness.freshness === 'error') {
66:     return true;
67:   }
68:   // False-safe contract (BUG-01 fix): block on ANY non-fresh graph, not just
69:   // `action === 'full_scan'`. A deleted-files-only `freshness:'stale', action:'none'`
70:   // result and a FAILED inline selective_reindex both previously fell through
71:   // to buildContext() and answered over a stale graph. Gate on freshness and a
72:   // failed gold-verification gate, matching query.ts + detect_changes.
73:   return readiness.freshness !== 'fresh' || readiness.verificationGate === 'fail';
74: }
75: 
76: function seedProvider(seed: NonNullable<ContextHandlerArgs['seeds']>[number]): string | undefined {
77:   return seed.provider;
78: }
79: 
80: function seedFilePath(seed: NonNullable<ContextHandlerArgs['seeds']>[number]): string {
81:   return seed.file ?? seed.filePath ?? seed.file_path ?? '';
82: }
83: 
84: function seedStartLine(seed: NonNullable<ContextHandlerArgs['seeds']>[number]): number | undefined {
85:   return seed.range?.start ?? seed.lines?.start ?? seed.startLine ?? seed.start_line;
86: }
87: 
88: function seedEndLine(seed: NonNullable<ContextHandlerArgs['seeds']>[number]): number | undefined {
89:   return seed.range?.end ?? seed.lines?.end ?? seed.endLine ?? seed.end_line ?? seedStartLine(seed);
90: }
91: 
92: function buildContextFallbackDecision(readiness: ContextReadiness): ContextFallbackDecision | null {
93:   // Mirror `code_graph_query.buildFallbackDecision` shape so callers see ONE
94:   // shared recovery vocabulary across all three handlers.
95:   // - readiness crash (`freshness: 'error'`) → fall back to `rg`
96:   // - full_scan required (no inline performed) → run `code_graph_scan`
97:   if (readiness.freshness === 'error') {
98:     return {
99:       nextTool: 'rg',
100:       reason: typeof readiness.error === 'string' && readiness.error.length > 0
101:         ? `readiness_check_crashed: ${readiness.error}`
102:         : 'readiness_check_crashed',
103:     };
104:   }
105: 
106:   if (readiness.freshness !== 'fresh') {
107:     return {
108:       nextTool: 'code_graph_scan',
109:       reason: readiness.action === 'selective_reindex' ? 'selective_reindex' : 'full_scan_required',
110:     };
111:   }
112: 
113:   return null;
114: }
115: 
116: function resolveDeadlineMs(profile: ContextArgs['profile']): number {
117:   switch (profile) {
118:     case 'quick':
119:       return 250;
120:     case 'debug':
121:       return 700;
122:     case 'research':
123:       return 900;
124:     default:
125:       return 400;
126:   }
127: }
128: 
129: function resolveSeedSource(args: ContextHandlerArgs, anchor: {
130:   filePath: string;
131:   startLine: number;
132:   endLine: number;
133:   symbolId: string | null;
134:   fqName: string | null;
135: }): string | undefined {
136:   const normalizedSeeds: NormalizedSeedSource[] = (args.seeds ?? []).map((seed) => ({
137:     source: typeof seed.source === 'string' && seed.source.trim().length > 0
138:       ? seed.source
139:       : seedProvider(seed),
140:     filePath: seedFilePath(seed),
141:     startLine: seedStartLine(seed),
142:     endLine: seedEndLine(seed),
143:     symbolId: seed.symbolId,
144:     symbolName: seed.symbolName,
145:   }));
146: 
147:   const match = normalizedSeeds.find((seed) => {
148:     if (seed.symbolId && anchor.symbolId) {
149:       return seed.symbolId === anchor.symbolId;
150:     }
151:     if (seed.symbolName && anchor.fqName) {
152:       return seed.symbolName === anchor.fqName || anchor.fqName.endsWith(`.${seed.symbolName}`);
153:     }
154:     if (seed.filePath !== anchor.filePath) {
155:       return false;
156:     }
157:     if (typeof seed.startLine === 'number' && seed.startLine !== anchor.startLine) {
158:       return false;
159:     }
160:     if (typeof seed.endLine === 'number' && seed.endLine !== anchor.endLine) {
161:       return false;
162:     }
163:     return true;
164:   });
165: 
166:   return match?.source;
167: }
168: 
169: /** Handle code_graph_context tool call */
170: export async function handleCodeGraphContext(args: ContextHandlerArgs): Promise<{ content: Array<{ type: string; text: string }> }> {
171:   let readiness: ContextReadiness = {
172:     freshness: 'empty' as const,
173:     action: 'none' as const,
174:     inlineIndexPerformed: false,
175:     reason: 'readiness check not run',
176:   };
177: 
178:   try {
179:     // Auto-trigger: ensure graph is fresh before querying
180:     try {
181:       readiness = await ensureCodeGraphReady(process.cwd(), {
182:         allowInlineIndex: true,
183:         allowInlineFullScan: false,
184:         allowGuardedInlineFullScan: true,
185:       });
186:     } catch (err: unknown) {
187:       // Surface as canonical 'error' freshness so buildReadinessBlock() maps
188:       // it to canonicalReadiness='missing' + trustState='unavailable'
189:       // automatically. Removes the manual trustState injection that previously
190:       // lived at the response site.
191:       readiness = {
192:         freshness: 'error' as const,
193:         action: 'none' as const,
194:         inlineIndexPerformed: false,
195:         reason: 'readiness_check_crashed',
196:         error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err),
197:       };
198:     }
199: 
200:     if (shouldBlockReadPath(readiness)) {
201:       const readinessBlock = buildReadinessBlock(readiness);
202:       const fallbackDecision = buildContextFallbackDecision(readiness);
203:       // Differentiate the crash-on-probe envelope from the standard
204:       // full_scan-required envelope so operators see WHY graph answers were
205:       // omitted. The `rg` fallback is the same recovery the query handler
206:       // already ships; this aligns the three handlers on one shared
207:       // degraded-readiness vocabulary.
208:       const isCrash = readiness.freshness === 'error';
209:       const message = isCrash
210:         ? `code_graph_not_ready: ${readiness.reason}`
211:         : `code_graph_full_scan_required: ${readiness.reason}`;
212:       const requiredAction = isCrash ? 'rg' : 'code_graph_scan';
213:       const blockReason = isCrash ? 'readiness_check_crashed' : 'full_scan_required';
214: 
215:       // graphDb.getStats() can throw if the DB itself is unavailable. Isolate
216:       // the call so the degraded envelope still ships even when stats fail.
217:       let lastPersistedAt: string | null = null;
218:       try {
219:         lastPersistedAt = graphDb.getStats().lastScanTimestamp;
220:       } catch {
221:         lastPersistedAt = null;
222:       }
223: 
224:       return {
225:         content: [{
226:           type: 'text',
227:           text: JSON.stringify({
228:             status: 'blocked',
229:             message,
230:             data: {
231:               queryMode: args.queryMode ?? 'neighborhood',
232:               blocked: true,
233:               degraded: true,
234:               graphAnswersOmitted: true,
235:               requiredAction,
236:               blockReason,
237:               // Surface scope + manifest diagnostics directly on
238:               // the blocked payload's `data` object
239:               // so operators can route on them without parsing
240:               // `data.readiness`. Backward compatible: legacy
241:               // callers reading `status` + `data.blockReason`
242:               // continue to work; nested `data.readiness` still
243:               // carries the same fields.
244:               reason: readiness.reason,
245:               activeScope: readiness.activeScope ?? null,
246:               storedScope: readiness.storedScope ?? null,
247:               manifestCount: readiness.manifestCount ?? null,
248:               manifestDigest: readiness.manifestDigest ?? null,
249:               readiness: readinessBlock,
250:               canonicalReadiness: readinessBlock.canonicalReadiness,
251:               trustState: readinessBlock.trustState,
252:               ...(fallbackDecision ? { fallbackDecision } : {}),
253:               lastPersistedAt,
254:             },
255:           }, null, 2),
256:         }],
257:       };
258:     }
259: 
260:     const queryMode = (['neighborhood', 'outline', 'impact'].includes(args.queryMode ?? '')
261:       ? args.queryMode as QueryMode
262:       : 'neighborhood');
263: 
264:     const seeds = (args.seeds ?? []).map((seed) => {
265:       const source = typeof seed.source === 'string' && seed.source.trim().length > 0
266:         ? seed.source
267:         : seedProvider(seed);
268:       const provider = seedProvider(seed);
269: 
270:       if (provider === 'manual' && seed.symbolName) {
271:         return {
272:           provider: 'manual' as const,
273:           symbolName: seed.symbolName,
274:           filePath: seed.filePath,
275:           kind: seed.kind,
276:           source,
277:         };
278:       }
279: 
280:       if (provider === 'graph' && seed.symbolId) {
281:         return {
282:           provider: 'graph' as const,
283:           nodeId: seed.nodeId ?? seed.symbolId,
284:           symbolId: seed.symbolId,
285:           source,
286:         };
287:       }
288: 
289:       return {
290:         filePath: seedFilePath(seed),
291:         startLine: seedStartLine(seed),
292:         endLine: seedEndLine(seed),
293:         query: seed.query,
294:         source,
295:       };
296:     }) as unknown as CodeGraphSeed[];
297: 
298:     const profile = (['quick', 'research', 'debug'].includes(args.profile ?? '') ? args.profile : undefined) as ContextArgs['profile'];
299:     const deadlineMs = resolveDeadlineMs(profile);
300: 
301:     const contextArgs: ContextArgs = {
302:       input: args.input,
303:       queryMode,
304:       subject: args.subject,
305:       seeds,
306:       budgetTokens: args.budgetTokens ?? 1200,
307:       deadlineMs,
308:       profile,
309:       includeTrace: args.includeTrace,
310:     };
311: 
312:     const result = buildContext(contextArgs);
313:     // trustState is now derived canonically by buildReadinessBlock() through
314:     // the widened freshness union ('error' → trustState 'unavailable'). No
315:     // manual injection needed.
316:     const readinessBlock = buildReadinessBlock(readiness);
317:     const lastPersistedAt = graphDb.getStats().lastScanTimestamp;
318: 
319:     return {
320:       content: [{
321:         type: 'text',
322:         text: JSON.stringify({
323:           status: 'ok',
324:           data: {
325:             queryMode: result.queryMode,
326:             combinedSummary: result.combinedSummary,
327:             nextActions: result.nextActions,
328:             readiness: readinessBlock,
329:             canonicalReadiness: readinessBlock.canonicalReadiness,
330:             trustState: readinessBlock.trustState,
331:             lastPersistedAt,
332:             anchors: result.resolvedAnchors.map(a => {
333:               const anchor: Record<string, unknown> = {
334:                 file: a.filePath,
335:                 line: a.startLine,
336:                 symbol: a.fqName,
337:                 resolution: a.resolution,
338:                 confidence: a.confidence,
339:                 source: resolveSeedSource(args, a) ?? a.source,
340:                 provider: a.provider,
341:                 score: a.score,
342:                 snippet: a.snippet,
343:                 range: a.range,
344:               };
345:               return anchor;
346:             }),
347:             graphContext: result.graphContext,
348:             textBrief: result.textBrief,
349:             metadata: result.metadata,
350:             graphMetadata: {
351:               detectorProvenance: graphDb.getLastDetectorProvenance() ?? 'unknown',
352:             },
353:           },
354:         }, null, 2),
355:       }],
356:     };
357:   } catch (err: unknown) {
358:     console.error('[code-graph-context] Unexpected failure:', err);
359:     return {
360:       content: [{
361:         type: 'text',
362:         text: JSON.stringify({
363:           status: 'error',
364:           error: 'code_graph_context failed',
365:         }),
366:       }],
367:     };
368:   }
369: }

(End of file - total 369 lines)

<content>
1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Code Graph Scan Handler
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // MCP tool handler for code_graph_scan — indexes workspace files.
5: 
6: import { execSync } from 'node:child_process';
7: import { existsSync } from 'node:fs';
8: import { basename, isAbsolute, relative, resolve } from 'node:path';
9: import { buildEdgeDistribution, computeEdgeShare } from '../lib/edge-drift.js';
10: import {
11:   hasCrossFileCallResolutionActivity,
12:   resolveCrossFileCallEdges,
13: } from '../lib/cross-file-edge-resolver.js';
14: import { getDefaultConfig, type DetectorProvenance, type CodeEdge } from '../lib/indexer-types.js';
15: import { indexFiles } from '../lib/structural-indexer.js';
16: import { resetParserHealth } from '../lib/tree-sitter-parser.js';
17: import * as graphDb from '../lib/code-graph-db.js';
18: import { persistIndexedFileResult, recordCandidateManifest } from '../lib/ensure-ready.js';
19: import {
20:   DEFAULT_GOLD_BATTERY_PATH,
21:   executeBattery,
22:   loadGoldBattery,
23:   type VerifyResult,
24: } from '../lib/gold-query-verifier.js';
25: import { isRecord } from '../lib/query-result-adapter.js';
26: import { buildReadinessBlock } from '../lib/readiness-contract.js';
27: import { canonicalizeWorkspacePaths, isWithinWorkspace } from '../lib/utils/workspace-path.js';
28: import { resolveIndexScopePolicy, scopeFingerprintsMatchOrLegacy } from '../lib/index-scope-policy.js';
29: import { getSkipListSummary } from '../lib/parser-skip-list.js';
30: import { handleCodeGraphQuery } from './query.js';
31: 
32: export interface ScanArgs {
33:   rootDir?: string;
34:   includeGlobs?: string[];
35:   excludeGlobs?: string[];
36:   incremental?: boolean;
37:   includeSkills?: boolean | string[];
38:   includeAgents?: boolean;
39:   includeCommands?: boolean;
40:   includeSpecs?: boolean;
41:   includePlugins?: boolean;
42:   verify?: boolean;
43:   persistBaseline?: boolean;
44:   forceZeroNodeReset?: boolean;
45:   forceScopeChange?: boolean;
46: }
47: 
48: export interface ScanResult {
49:   filesScanned: number;
50:   filesIndexed: number;
51:   filesSkipped: number;
52:   parserSkipListBypassCount: number;
53:   totalNodes: number;
54:   totalEdges: number;
55:   errors: string[];
56:   durationMs: number;
57:   fullScanRequested: boolean;
58:   effectiveIncremental: boolean;
59:   fullReindexTriggered?: boolean;
60:   currentGitHead?: string | null;
61:   previousGitHead?: string | null;
62:   detectorProvenanceSummary?: graphDb.DetectorProvenanceSummary;
63:   graphEdgeEnrichmentSummary?: graphDb.GraphEdgeEnrichmentSummary | null;
64:   parseDiagnostics: graphDb.ParseDiagnosticsSummary;
65:   parserSkipList: {
66:     added: number;
67:     healed: number;
68:     totalAfterScan: number;
69:   };
70:   staleButValidGraphFiles: number;
71:   failedScan?: graphDb.FailedScanRecord | null;
72:   warnings: string[];
73:   capExceeded: {
74:     maxNodes: boolean;
75:     depth: boolean;
76:     gitignoreSize: boolean;
77:   };
78:   verification?: VerifyResult;
79: }
80: 
81: const DEFAULT_FATAL_PARSE_ERROR_RATIO = 0.5;
82: const PARSER_SKIP_LIST_PREFIX = 'Parser skipped by skip-list';
83: 
84: function summarizeDetectorProvenance(
85:   results: Array<{ detectorProvenance?: DetectorProvenance }>,
86: ): graphDb.DetectorProvenanceSummary {
87:   const counts: Partial<Record<DetectorProvenance, number>> = {};
88:   let dominant: DetectorProvenance | 'unknown' = 'unknown';
89:   let dominantCount = 0;
90: 
91:   for (const result of results) {
92:     if (!result.detectorProvenance) {
93:       continue;
94:     }
95:     const nextCount = (counts[result.detectorProvenance] ?? 0) + 1;
96:     counts[result.detectorProvenance] = nextCount;
97:     if (nextCount > dominantCount) {
98:       dominantCount = nextCount;
99:       dominant = result.detectorProvenance;
100:     }
101:   }
102: 
103:   return { dominant, counts };
104: }
105: 
106: function summarizeGraphEdgeEnrichment(
107:   results: Array<{ edges: CodeEdge[] }>,
108: ): graphDb.GraphEdgeEnrichmentSummary | null {
109:   let best: graphDb.GraphEdgeEnrichmentSummary | null = null;
110: 
111:   for (const result of results) {
112:     for (const edge of result.edges) {
113:       const metadata = edge.metadata;
114:       if (!metadata || typeof metadata.confidence !== 'number') {
115:         continue;
116:       }
117: 
118:       const edgeEvidenceClass = (() => {
119:         switch (edge.edgeType) {
120:           case 'IMPORTS':
121:           case 'EXPORTS':
122:             return 'import' as const;
123:           case 'EXTENDS':
124:           case 'IMPLEMENTS':
125:           case 'TYPE_OF':
126:             return 'type_reference' as const;
127:           case 'TESTED_BY':
128:             return 'test_coverage' as const;
129:           default:
130:             return metadata.detectorProvenance === 'heuristic' || metadata.evidenceClass === 'INFERRED'
131:               ? 'inferred_heuristic' as const
132:               : 'direct_call' as const;
133:         }
134:       })();
135: 
136:       if (!best || metadata.confidence > best.numericConfidence) {
137:         best = {
138:           edgeEvidenceClass,
139:           numericConfidence: metadata.confidence,
140:         };
141:       }
142:     }
143:   }
144: 
145:   return best;
146: }
147: 
148: interface ParseErrorFileClassification {
149:   cleanCount: number;
150:   skipListBypassCount: number;
151:   realErrorCount: number;
152: }
153: 
154: function classifyParseErrorFiles(
155:   results: Array<{ parseHealth: string; parseErrors: string[] }>,
156: ): ParseErrorFileClassification {
157:   return results.reduce<ParseErrorFileClassification>((counts, result) => {
158:     const hasParseErrors = result.parseErrors.length > 0;
159:     const hasOnlySkipListBypassErrors = hasParseErrors
160:       && result.parseErrors.every((error) => error.startsWith(PARSER_SKIP_LIST_PREFIX));
161:     const hasRealParseErrors = result.parseErrors.some((error) => !error.startsWith(PARSER_SKIP_LIST_PREFIX));
162: 
163:     if (hasOnlySkipListBypassErrors) {
164:       counts.skipListBypassCount++;
165:     } else if (result.parseHealth === 'error' || hasRealParseErrors) {
166:       counts.realErrorCount++;
167:     } else {
168:       counts.cleanCount++;
169:     }
170: 
171:     return counts;
172:   }, {
173:     cleanCount: 0,
174:     skipListBypassCount: 0,
175:     realErrorCount: 0,
176:   });
177: }
178: 
179: function countPersistableNodes(results: Array<{ parseHealth: string; nodes: unknown[] }>): number {
180:   return results.reduce((total, result) => (
181:     result.parseHealth === 'error' ? total : total + result.nodes.length
182:   ), 0);
183: }
184: 
185: function computeParseErrorRatio(parseErrorCount: number, totalFiles: number): number {
186:   if (totalFiles === 0) {
187:     return 0;
188:   }
189:   return parseErrorCount / totalFiles;
190: }
191: 
192: function formatParseDiagnosticMessage(result: { parseErrors: string[] }): string {
193:   return result.parseErrors.length > 0
194:     ? result.parseErrors.join('; ')
195:     : 'Parser returned parseHealth="error" without diagnostics';
196: }
197: 
198: function recordParseDiagnosticsForResults(
199:   results: Array<{ filePath: string; parseHealth: string; parseErrors: string[] }>,
200: ): void {
201:   for (const result of results) {
202:     if (result.parseHealth !== 'error' && result.parseErrors.length === 0) {
203:       continue;
204:     }
205:     graphDb.recordParseDiagnostic(result.filePath, formatParseDiagnosticMessage(result));
206:   }
207: }
208: 
209: function getCurrentGitHead(rootDir: string): string | null {
210:   try {
211:     return execSync('git rev-parse HEAD', {
212:       cwd: rootDir,
213:       encoding: 'utf-8',
214:       timeout: 5_000,
215:       stdio: ['ignore', 'pipe', 'pipe'],
216:     }).trim();
217:   } catch (err: unknown) {
218:     const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
219:     console.error(`[code-graph-scan] Failed to read git HEAD for ${rootDir}: ${message}`);
220:     return null;
221:   }
222: }
223: 
224: function cleanupMissingTrackedFiles(filePaths: string[]): void {
225:   for (const filePath of filePaths) {
226:     if (existsSync(filePath)) {
227:       continue;
228:     }
229: 
230:     graphDb.removeFile(filePath);
231:   }
232: }
233: 
234: function summarizeEdgeDistribution(results: Array<{ edges: CodeEdge[] }>) {
235:   const edgeCounts = buildEdgeDistribution();
236: 
237:   for (const result of results) {
238:     for (const edge of result.edges) {
239:       edgeCounts[edge.edgeType] += 1;
240:     }
241:   }
242: 
243:   return computeEdgeShare(edgeCounts);
244: }
245: 
246: function hasUsablePersistedEdgeDistributionBaseline(): boolean {
247:   const rawBaseline = graphDb.getCodeGraphMetadata('edge_distribution_baseline');
248:   if (!rawBaseline) {
249:     return false;
250:   }
251: 
252:   try {
253:     const parsedBaseline = JSON.parse(rawBaseline) as unknown;
254:     if (!isRecord(parsedBaseline)) {
255:       return false;
256:     }
257: 
258:     buildEdgeDistribution(parsedBaseline);
259:     return true;
260:   } catch {
261:     return false;
262:   }
263: }
264: 
265: function relativize(absPath: string, workspaceRoot: string): string {
266:   const resolvedPath = resolve(absPath);
267:   const resolvedWorkspace = resolve(workspaceRoot);
268:   const workspaceRelative = relative(resolvedWorkspace, resolvedPath);
269:   if (workspaceRelative === '') {
270:     return '.';
271:   }
272:   if (!workspaceRelative.startsWith('..') && !isAbsolute(workspaceRelative)) {
273:     return workspaceRelative;
274:   }
275:   return basename(resolvedPath);
276: }
277: 
278: const PATH_DELIMITERS = /([\s:'"`{}\[\],()\x00]+)/;
279: 
280: function relativizeScanMessage(message: string, workspaceRoot: string): string {
281:   return message.split(PATH_DELIMITERS).map(segment => {
282:     if (segment.startsWith('/')) {
283:       return relativize(segment, workspaceRoot);
284:     }
285:     return segment;
286:   }).join('');
287: }
288: 
289: function relativizeScanWarning(warning: string, workspaceRoot: string): string {
290:   return relativizeScanMessage(warning, workspaceRoot);
291: }
292: 
293: export function relativizeScanError(error: string, workspaceRoot: string): string {
294:   return relativizeScanMessage(error, workspaceRoot);
295: }
296: 
297: function relativizeParseDiagnostics(
298:   summary: graphDb.ParseDiagnosticsSummary,
299:   workspaceRoot: string,
300: ): graphDb.ParseDiagnosticsSummary {
301:   return {
302:     affectedFiles: summary.affectedFiles,
303:     recentErrors: summary.recentErrors.map((diagnostic) => ({
304:       ...diagnostic,
305:       filePath: relativize(diagnostic.filePath, workspaceRoot),
306:       errorMessage: relativizeScanError(diagnostic.errorMessage, workspaceRoot),
307:     })),
308:   };
309: }
310: 
311: /** Handle code_graph_scan tool call */
312: export async function handleCodeGraphScan(args: ScanArgs): Promise<{ content: Array<{ type: string; text: string }> }> {
313:   const startTime = Date.now();
314:   const rootDir = args.rootDir ?? process.cwd();
315:   const incremental = args.incremental !== false;
316:   const resolvedRootDir = resolve(rootDir);
317: 
318:   // SECURITY: Canonicalize paths via realpathSync() to prevent symlink bypass.
319:   // A symlink inside the workspace pointing outside it would pass a lexical
320:   // startsWith() check on the resolved (but not canonicalized) path.
321:   const canonical = canonicalizeWorkspacePaths(resolvedRootDir);
322:   if (!canonical) {
323:     // Broken symlink or non-existent path — reject immediately
324:     return {
325:       content: [{
326:         type: 'text',
327:         text: JSON.stringify({
328:           status: 'error',
329:           error: `rootDir path is invalid or contains a broken symlink: ${relativize(resolvedRootDir, process.cwd())}`,
330:         }),
331:       }],
332:     };
333:   }
334:   const { canonicalWorkspace, canonicalRootDir } = canonical;
335: 
336:   if (!isWithinWorkspace(canonicalWorkspace, canonicalRootDir)) {
337:     return {
338:       content: [{
339:         type: 'text',
340:         text: JSON.stringify({
341:           status: 'error',
342:           error: `rootDir must stay within the workspace root; received: ${relativize(canonicalRootDir, canonicalWorkspace)}`,
343:         }),
344:       }],
345:     };
346:   }
347: 
348:   const scopePolicy = resolveIndexScopePolicy({
349:     includeSkills: args.includeSkills,
350:     includeAgents: args.includeAgents,
351:     includeCommands: args.includeCommands,
352:     includeSpecs: args.includeSpecs,
353:     includePlugins: args.includePlugins,
354:     includeGlobs: args.includeGlobs,
355:     excludeGlobs: args.excludeGlobs,
356:   });
357:   const config = getDefaultConfig(canonicalRootDir, scopePolicy);
358:   const initialSkipListCount = getSkipListSummary().count;
359: 
360:   const previousGitHead = graphDb.getLastGitHead();
361:   const currentGitHead = getCurrentGitHead(canonicalRootDir);
362:   const gitHeadChanged = previousGitHead !== null
363:     && currentGitHead !== null
364:     && previousGitHead !== currentGitHead;
365:   const effectiveIncremental = incremental;
366:   const fullReindexTriggered = gitHeadChanged && !effectiveIncremental;
367: 
368:   if (gitHeadChanged && incremental) {
369:     console.error(`[code-graph-scan] Git HEAD changed (${previousGitHead} -> ${currentGitHead}); honoring incremental content-hash reindex`);
370:   }
371: 
372:   // An explicit full scan is a deliberate retry, so clear any prior global parser
373:   // quarantine before indexing. A B2 ("memory access out of bounds") quarantine
374:   // otherwise persists until a launcher restart, making every full scan return
375:   // zero nodes (then rejected by the zero-node guard below). resetParserHealth()
376:   // drops the corrupted parser instance so indexing re-initializes a fresh
377:   // web-tree-sitter instance. Incremental scans do NOT reset — only an explicit
378:   // `incremental: false` request signals intent to retry.
379:   if (args.incremental === false) {
380:     resetParserHealth();
381:   }
382: 
383:   const results = await indexFiles(config, { skipFreshFiles: effectiveIncremental });
384:   const detectorProvenanceSummary = summarizeDetectorProvenance(results);
385:   let graphEdgeEnrichmentSummary = summarizeGraphEdgeEnrichment(results);
386:   const preParseSkippedCount = effectiveIncremental ? (results.preParseSkippedCount ?? 0) : 0;
387:   const priorStats = graphDb.getStats();
388:   const priorNodeCount = priorStats.totalNodes;
389:   const candidatePersistableNodeCount = countPersistableNodes(results);
390:   const parseErrorClassification = classifyParseErrorFiles(results);
391:   const parseErrorCount = parseErrorClassification.realErrorCount;
392:   const parserSkipListBypassCount = parseErrorClassification.skipListBypassCount;
393:   // Skip-list bypasses are intentionally not parsed, so exclude them from the
394:   // ratio denominator. The fatal threshold should measure degradation among
395:   // files the parser actually attempted, especially in narrow incremental scans.
396:   const parseErrorRatio = computeParseErrorRatio(parseErrorCount, results.length - parserSkipListBypassCount);
397:   const severeParseErrorScan = parseErrorRatio > DEFAULT_FATAL_PARSE_ERROR_RATIO;
398:   const fullScan = !effectiveIncremental;
399:   const storedScope = graphDb.getStoredCodeGraphScope();
400:   const candidateFingerprint = scopePolicy.fingerprint;
401:   const scopeChangePromotionBlocked = fullScan
402:     && priorStats.totalNodes > 0
403:     && storedScope?.fingerprint
404:     && !scopeFingerprintsMatchOrLegacy(storedScope.fingerprint, candidateFingerprint)
405:     && args.forceScopeChange !== true;
406:   const zeroNodePromotionBlocked = fullScan
407:     && candidatePersistableNodeCount === 0
408:     && priorNodeCount > 0
409:     && args.forceZeroNodeReset !== true;
410: 
411:   if (scopeChangePromotionBlocked) {
412:     recordParseDiagnosticsForResults(results);
413:     const reason = 'scope_change_scan_rejected';
414:     const failedScan = graphDb.recordFailedScan({
415:       reason,
416:       totalFiles: results.length,
417:       totalNodes: candidatePersistableNodeCount,
418:       parseErrorCount,
419:       parseErrorRatio,
420:       previousGitHead,
421:       currentGitHead,
422:       scopeFingerprint: scopePolicy.fingerprint,
423:       scopeLabel: scopePolicy.label,
424:       errors: results.flatMap((result) => result.parseErrors).slice(0, 10),
425:     });
426:     console.warn(
427:       `[code-graph-scan] Blocked scope-change full scan promotion over existing graph (${priorNodeCount} prior node(s)); stored scope ${storedScope.fingerprint} differs from candidate scope ${candidateFingerprint}; pass forceScopeChange: true to allow scope replacement.`,
428:     );
429:     const parseDiagnostics = relativizeParseDiagnostics(graphDb.getParseDiagnosticsSummary(), canonicalWorkspace);
430:     const skipListSummary = getSkipListSummary();
431:     const readinessBlock = buildReadinessBlock({
432:       freshness: 'stale',
433:       action: 'full_scan',
434:       inlineIndexPerformed: false,
435:       reason: 'scope-change scan rejected to preserve existing graph state',
436:     });
437: 
438:     return {
439:       content: [{
440:         type: 'text',
441:         text: JSON.stringify({
442:           status: 'blocked',
443:           reason,
444:           data: {
445:             filesScanned: results.length,
446:             filesIndexed: 0,
447:             filesSkipped: preParseSkippedCount,
448:             parserSkipListBypassCount,
449:             totalNodes: priorStats.totalNodes,
450:             totalEdges: priorStats.totalEdges,
451:             errors: results.flatMap((result) => {
452:               const filePath = relativize(result.filePath, canonicalWorkspace);
453:               return result.parseErrors.map((error) => `${filePath}: ${relativizeScanError(error, canonicalWorkspace)}`);
454:             }).slice(0, 10),
455:             durationMs: Date.now() - startTime,
456:             fullScanRequested: args.incremental === false,
457:             effectiveIncremental,
458:             fullReindexTriggered,
459:             currentGitHead,
460:             previousGitHead,
461:             detectorProvenanceSummary,
462:             graphEdgeEnrichmentSummary,
463:             parseDiagnostics,
464:             parserSkipList: {
465:               added: Math.max(0, skipListSummary.count - initialSkipListCount),
466:               healed: 0,
467:               totalAfterScan: skipListSummary.count,
468:             },
469:             staleButValidGraphFiles: graphDb.countStaleButValidParseDiagnostics(),
470:             failedScan,
471:             warnings: [
472:               `scope-change scan rejected; existing graph has ${priorNodeCount} node(s)`,
473:               `stored scope fingerprint ${storedScope.fingerprint} differs from candidate scope fingerprint ${candidateFingerprint}; pass forceScopeChange: true if intentional`,
474:               ...(results.warnings ?? []).map(warning => relativizeScanWarning(warning, canonicalWorkspace)),
475:             ],
476:             capExceeded: results.capExceeded ?? { maxNodes: false, depth: false, gitignoreSize: false },
477:             readiness: readinessBlock,
478:             canonicalReadiness: readinessBlock.canonicalReadiness,
479:             trustState: readinessBlock.trustState,
480:             lastPersistedAt: priorStats.lastScanTimestamp,
481:           },
482:         }, null, 2),
483:       }],
484:     };
485:   }
486: 
487:   if (zeroNodePromotionBlocked) {
488:     recordParseDiagnosticsForResults(results);
489:     const reason = 'zero_node_scan_rejected';
490:     const failedScan = graphDb.recordFailedScan({
491:       reason,
492:       totalFiles: results.length,
493:       totalNodes: candidatePersistableNodeCount,
494:       parseErrorCount,
495:       parseErrorRatio,
496:       previousGitHead,
497:       currentGitHead,
498:       scopeFingerprint: scopePolicy.fingerprint,
499:       scopeLabel: scopePolicy.label,
500:       errors: results.flatMap((result) => result.parseErrors).slice(0, 10),
501:     });
502:     console.warn(
503:       `[code-graph-scan] Blocked zero-node full scan promotion over existing graph (${priorNodeCount} prior node(s)); pass forceZeroNodeReset:true to allow destructive reset.`,
504:     );
505:     const parseDiagnostics = relativizeParseDiagnostics(graphDb.getParseDiagnosticsSummary(), canonicalWorkspace);
506:     const skipListSummary = getSkipListSummary();
507:     const readinessBlock = buildReadinessBlock({
508:       freshness: 'stale',
509:       action: 'full_scan',
510:       inlineIndexPerformed: false,
511:       reason: 'zero-node scan rejected to preserve existing graph state',
512:     });
513: 
514:     return {
515:       content: [{
516:         type: 'text',
517:         text: JSON.stringify({
518:           status: 'blocked',
519:           reason,
520:           data: {
521:             filesScanned: results.length,
522:             filesIndexed: 0,
523:             filesSkipped: preParseSkippedCount,
524:             parserSkipListBypassCount,
525:             totalNodes: priorStats.totalNodes,
526:             totalEdges: priorStats.totalEdges,
527:             errors: results.flatMap((result) => {
528:               const filePath = relativize(result.filePath, canonicalWorkspace);
529:               return result.parseErrors.map((error) => `${filePath}: ${relativizeScanError(error, canonicalWorkspace)}`);
530:             }).slice(0, 10),
531:             durationMs: Date.now() - startTime,
532:             fullScanRequested: args.incremental === false,
533:             effectiveIncremental,
534:             fullReindexTriggered,
535:             currentGitHead,
536:             previousGitHead,
537:             detectorProvenanceSummary,
538:             graphEdgeEnrichmentSummary,
539:             parseDiagnostics,
540:             parserSkipList: {
541:               added: Math.max(0, skipListSummary.count - initialSkipListCount),
542:               healed: 0,
543:               totalAfterScan: skipListSummary.count,
544:             },
545:             staleButValidGraphFiles: graphDb.countStaleButValidParseDiagnostics(),
546:             failedScan,
547:             warnings: [
548:               `zero-node scan rejected; existing graph has ${priorNodeCount} node(s)`,
549:               ...(results.warnings ?? []).map(warning => relativizeScanWarning(warning, canonicalWorkspace)),
550:             ],
551:             capExceeded: results.capExceeded ?? { maxNodes: false, depth: false, gitignoreSize: false },
552:             readiness: readinessBlock,
553:             canonicalReadiness: readinessBlock.canonicalReadiness,
554:             trustState: readinessBlock.trustState,
555:             lastPersistedAt: priorStats.lastScanTimestamp,
556:           },
557:         }, null, 2),
558:       }],
559:     };
560:   }
561: 
562:   let filesIndexed = 0;
563:   let filesSkipped = preParseSkippedCount;
564:   let totalNodes = 0;
565:   let totalEdges = 0;
566:   const errors: string[] = [];
567:   const structuralErrors: string[] = [];
568:   const parserSkipWarnings: string[] = [];
569: 
570:   if (effectiveIncremental) {
571:     cleanupMissingTrackedFiles(graphDb.getTrackedFiles());
572:   } else if (!severeParseErrorScan) {
573:     const indexedPaths = new Set(results.map((result) => result.filePath));
574:     for (const filePath of graphDb.getTrackedFiles()) {
575:       if (!indexedPaths.has(filePath)) {
576:         graphDb.removeFile(filePath);
577:       }
578:     }
579:   }
580: 
581:   for (const result of results) {
582:     // Skip unchanged files in incremental mode
583:     if (
584:       effectiveIncremental
585:       && !graphDb.isFileStale(
586:         result.filePath,
587:         result.contentHash ? { currentContentHash: result.contentHash } : undefined,
588:       )
589:     ) {
590:       filesSkipped++;
591:       continue;
592:     }
593: 
594:     try {
595:       // BUG-03: defer the dangling-target edge prune per-file. A full scan
596:       // persists files one at a time, so a cross-file IMPORTS edge whose target
597:       // lives in a not-yet-persisted file would be pruned here and never
598:       // restored. We sweep once with pruneDanglingEdges() after the loop and
599:       // cross-file resolution, when every target node exists.
600:       persistIndexedFileResult(result, { deferDanglingTargetPrune: true });
601: 
602:       if (result.parseHealth !== 'error') {
603:         filesIndexed++;
604:         totalNodes += result.nodes.length;
605:         totalEdges += result.edges.length;
606:       }
607:     } catch (err: unknown) {
608:       const filePath = relativize(result.filePath, canonicalWorkspace);
609:       const message = `${filePath}: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}`;
610:       errors.push(message);
611:       structuralErrors.push(message);
612:     }
613: 
614:     if (result.parseErrors.length > 0) {
615:       const filePath = relativize(result.filePath, canonicalWorkspace);
616:       // Parser skip-list entries are the designed safety net for grammars that
617:       // crash on certain inputs (B1: tree-sitter API quirk; B2: WASM trap).
618:       // Route them to warnings so they do not pollute the error count or
619:       // obscure real persistence failures in failedScan.errors.
620:       for (const parseError of result.parseErrors) {
621:         const message = `${filePath}: ${parseError}`;
622:         if (parseError.includes('Parser skipped by skip-list')) {
623:           parserSkipWarnings.push(message);
624:         } else {
625:           errors.push(message);
626:         }
627:       }
628:     }
629:   }
630: 
631:   const scanPromotable = !severeParseErrorScan && structuralErrors.length === 0;
632:   const failedScanErrors = [
633:     ...structuralErrors,
634:     ...errors.filter(error => !structuralErrors.includes(error)),
635:   ].slice(0, 10);
636:   const failedScan = scanPromotable
637:     ? null
638:     : graphDb.recordFailedScan({
639:       reason: severeParseErrorScan ? 'parse_error_threshold_exceeded' : 'structural_persistence_error',
640:       totalFiles: results.length,
641:       totalNodes: candidatePersistableNodeCount,
642:       parseErrorCount,
643:       parseErrorRatio,
644:       previousGitHead,
645:       currentGitHead,
646:       scopeFingerprint: scopePolicy.fingerprint,
647:       scopeLabel: scopePolicy.label,
648:       errors: failedScanErrors,
649:     });
650: 
651:   if (scanPromotable && filesIndexed > 0 && results.length > 0) {
652:     graphDb.setLastDetectorProvenance(results[0].detectorProvenance);
653:   }
654: 
655:   if (scanPromotable && currentGitHead) {
656:     graphDb.setLastGitHead(currentGitHead);
657:   }
658:   if (scanPromotable && detectorProvenanceSummary.dominant !== 'unknown') {
659:     graphDb.setLastDetectorProvenance(detectorProvenanceSummary.dominant);
660:   }
661:   if (scanPromotable) {
662:     graphDb.setLastDetectorProvenanceSummary(detectorProvenanceSummary);
663:     graphDb.setCodeGraphScope(scopePolicy);
664:   }
665: 
666:   // Refresh candidate manifest after a successful scan so
667:   // the next detectState() has a current baseline to compare against. Without
668:   // this, code_graph_status reports stale ("candidate manifest drift") on the
669:   // very next call after an explicit user-triggered scan. Incremental scans
670:   // also discover new indexable files via find-candidates, so refresh their
671:   // manifest after successful promotion too.
672:   if (scanPromotable) {
673:     try {
674:       recordCandidateManifest(graphDb.getTrackedFiles());
675:     } catch {
676:       // Best-effort: manifest recording must never block a successful scan
677:     }
678:   }
679: 
680:   const hasPersistedBaseline = hasUsablePersistedEdgeDistributionBaseline();
681:   if (
682:     !effectiveIncremental
683:     && scanPromotable
684:     && (!hasPersistedBaseline || args.persistBaseline === true)
685:   ) {
686:     const distribution = summarizeEdgeDistribution(results);
687:     graphDb.setCodeGraphMetadata('edge_distribution_baseline', JSON.stringify(distribution));
688:   }
689: 
690:   const crossFileCallResolution = filesIndexed > 0 && scanPromotable
691:     ? resolveCrossFileCallEdges()
692:     : { resolved: 0, unresolved: 0, ambiguousSkipped: 0 };
693:   if (hasCrossFileCallResolutionActivity(crossFileCallResolution)) {
694:     graphEdgeEnrichmentSummary = {
695:       ...(graphEdgeEnrichmentSummary ?? {
696:         edgeEvidenceClass: 'inferred_heuristic' as const,
697:         numericConfidence: 0.8,
698:       }),
699:       crossFileCallResolution,
700:     };
701:   }
702: 
703:   if (scanPromotable && filesIndexed > 0 && graphEdgeEnrichmentSummary) {
704:     graphDb.setLastGraphEdgeEnrichmentSummary(graphEdgeEnrichmentSummary);
705:   } else if (scanPromotable && filesIndexed > 0) {
706:     graphDb.clearLastGraphEdgeEnrichmentSummary();
707:   }
708: 
709:   // BUG-03: now that every file's nodes are persisted and cross-file CALL
710:   // edges are resolved, sweep genuinely-dangling edges ONCE. Per-file
711:   // replaceEdges deferred this prune so forward-referenced cross-file IMPORTS
712:   // edges (importer persisted before the imported file) survived; here their
713:   // targets exist, so only truly-orphaned edges are removed.
714:   if (filesIndexed > 0) {
715:     graphDb.pruneDanglingEdges();
716:   }
717: 
718:   // FIX-011-FOLLOWUP-1: report POST-PERSIST DB counts so the scan response
719:   // matches what the next code_graph_status will see. The pre-persist sums
720:   // (totalNodes/totalEdges accumulated above) double-count edges that get
721:   // deduped during persistence and miss edges added by enrichment that runs
722:   // before the response is built — leading to a confusing ~1k delta between
723:   // scan response and immediate status. The DB read is cheap (2 COUNT(*)
724:   // queries) and gives a single source of truth for graph cardinality.
725:   const persistedStats = graphDb.getStats();
726:   const responseTotalNodes = persistedStats.totalNodes;
727:   const responseTotalEdges = persistedStats.totalEdges;
728:   const parseDiagnostics = relativizeParseDiagnostics(graphDb.getParseDiagnosticsSummary(), canonicalWorkspace);
729:   const skipListSummary = getSkipListSummary();
730: 
731:   const scanResult: ScanResult = {
732:     filesScanned: results.length,
733:     filesIndexed,
734:     filesSkipped,
735:     parserSkipListBypassCount,
736:     totalNodes: responseTotalNodes,
737:     totalEdges: responseTotalEdges,
738:     errors: errors.slice(0, 10).map(error => relativizeScanError(error, canonicalWorkspace)),
739:     durationMs: Date.now() - startTime,
740:     fullScanRequested: args.incremental === false,
741:     effectiveIncremental,
742:     fullReindexTriggered,
743:     currentGitHead,
744:     previousGitHead,
745:     detectorProvenanceSummary,
746:     graphEdgeEnrichmentSummary,
747:     parseDiagnostics,
748:     parserSkipList: {
749:       added: Math.max(0, skipListSummary.count - initialSkipListCount),
750:       healed: 0,
751:       totalAfterScan: skipListSummary.count,
752:     },
753:     staleButValidGraphFiles: graphDb.countStaleButValidParseDiagnostics(),
754:     failedScan,
755:     warnings: [
756:       ...(severeParseErrorScan
757:         ? [`scan metadata promotion blocked: real parse error ratio ${parseErrorRatio.toFixed(2)} exceeds ${DEFAULT_FATAL_PARSE_ERROR_RATIO}`]
758:         : []),
759:       ...parserSkipWarnings.slice(0, 10),
760:       ...(results.warnings ?? []).map(warning => relativizeScanWarning(warning, canonicalWorkspace)),
761:     ],
762:     capExceeded: results.capExceeded ?? { maxNodes: false, depth: false, gitignoreSize: false },
763:   };
764:   const lastPersistedAt = persistedStats.lastScanTimestamp;
765:   const shouldVerify = args.verify === true && incremental === false;
766: 
767:   if (shouldVerify) {
768:     const verification = {
769:       ...(await executeBattery(loadGoldBattery(DEFAULT_GOLD_BATTERY_PATH), handleCodeGraphQuery)),
770:       batteryPath: DEFAULT_GOLD_BATTERY_PATH,
771:     };
772:     graphDb.setLastGoldVerification(verification);
773:     scanResult.verification = verification;
774:   }
775: 
776:   const readinessBlock = buildReadinessBlock({
777:     freshness: lastPersistedAt ? 'fresh' : 'empty',
778:     action: fullReindexTriggered || !effectiveIncremental ? 'full_scan' : 'selective_reindex',
779:     inlineIndexPerformed: true,
780:     reason: lastPersistedAt
781:       ? 'scan completed and persisted current graph state'
782:       : 'scan completed but no graph data was persisted',
783:   });
784: 
785:   return {
786:     content: [{
787:       type: 'text',
788:       text: JSON.stringify({
789:         status: 'ok',
790:         data: {
791:           ...scanResult,
792:           readiness: readinessBlock,
793:           canonicalReadiness: readinessBlock.canonicalReadiness,
794:           trustState: readinessBlock.trustState,
795:           lastPersistedAt,
796:         },
797:       }, null, 2),
798:     }],
799:   };
800: }

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Code Graph Status Handler
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // MCP tool handler for code_graph_status — reports graph health.
5: 
6: import * as graphDb from '../lib/code-graph-db.js';
7: import {
8:   EDGE_DRIFT_JSD_THRESHOLD,
9:   EDGE_DRIFT_PSI_THRESHOLD,
10:   EDGE_DRIFT_SHARE_THRESHOLD,
11:   buildEdgeDistribution,
12:   computeEdgeShare,
13:   computeJSD,
14:   computePSI,
15:   type EdgeDistribution,
16: } from '../lib/edge-drift.js';
17: import { getGraphFreshness, getGraphReadinessSnapshot } from '../lib/ensure-ready.js';
18: import {
19:   parseIndexScopePolicyFromFingerprint,
20:   resolveIndexScopePolicy,
21: } from '../lib/index-scope-policy.js';
22: import { isRecord } from '../lib/query-result-adapter.js';
23: import { buildReadinessBlock, type SharedPayloadTrustState } from '../lib/readiness-contract.js';
24: import { getSkipListSummary } from '../lib/parser-skip-list.js';
25: import { getParserHealth } from '../lib/tree-sitter-parser.js';
26: import { getLastApplyMetadata } from '../lib/apply-metadata.js';
27: 
28: /**
29:  * Gold-query verification trust state, derived from the persisted gold battery
30:  * execution results. Narrower than {@link SharedPayloadTrustState} (excludes
31:  * `unavailable`) because gold verification can only report `live`, `stale`, or
32:  * `absent` — it is not a liveness-dependent subsystem.
33:  *
34:  * @deprecated Use {@link SharedPayloadTrustState} from readiness-contract for
35:  * new code; this alias exists only for backward compatibility with status
36:  * handler consumers that reference `GoldVerificationTrust`.
37:  */
38: type GoldVerificationTrust = SharedPayloadTrustState;
39: 
40: interface VerificationPassPolicy {
41:   overall_pass_rate: number;
42:   edge_focus_pass_rate: number;
43: }
44: 
45: interface EdgeDriftSummary {
46:   share_drift: Record<string, number>;
47:   psi: number;
48:   jsd: number;
49:   flagged: boolean;
50: }
51: 
52: const GOLD_VERIFICATION_MAX_AGE_MS = 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;
53: 
54: function getParseDiagnosticsForStatus(): graphDb.ParseDiagnosticsSummary {
55:   if (typeof graphDb.getParseDiagnosticsSummary !== 'function') {
56:     return { affectedFiles: 0, recentErrors: [] };
57:   }
58:   return graphDb.getParseDiagnosticsSummary();
59: }
60: 
61: function getStaleButValidGraphFilesForStatus(): number {
62:   if (typeof graphDb.countStaleButValidParseDiagnostics !== 'function') {
63:     return 0;
64:   }
65:   return graphDb.countStaleButValidParseDiagnostics();
66: }
67: 
68: function getLastFailedScanForStatus(): graphDb.FailedScanRecord | null {
69:   if (typeof graphDb.getLastFailedScan !== 'function') {
70:     return null;
71:   }
72:   return graphDb.getLastFailedScan();
73: }
74: 
75: function getPersistedEdgeDistributionBaseline(): EdgeDistribution | null {
76:   const rawBaseline = graphDb.getCodeGraphMetadata('edge_distribution_baseline');
77:   if (!rawBaseline) {
78:     return null;
79:   }
80: 
81:   try {
82:     const parsedBaseline = JSON.parse(rawBaseline) as unknown;
83:     if (!isRecord(parsedBaseline)) {
84:       return null;
85:     }
86: 
87:     return buildEdgeDistribution(parsedBaseline);
88:   } catch {
89:     return null;
90:   }
91: }
92: 
93: function buildEdgeDriftSummary(edgesByType: Record<string, number>): EdgeDriftSummary | null {
94:   const baselineShare = getPersistedEdgeDistributionBaseline();
95:   if (!baselineShare) {
96:     return null;
97:   }
98: 
99:   const currentShare = computeEdgeShare(buildEdgeDistribution(edgesByType));
100:   const shareDrift = Object.fromEntries(
101:     Object.keys(currentShare).map((edgeType) => [
102:       edgeType,
103:       currentShare[edgeType as keyof typeof currentShare]
104:       - baselineShare[edgeType as keyof typeof baselineShare],
105:     ]),
106:   ) as Record<string, number>;
107:   const psi = computePSI(currentShare, baselineShare);
108:   const jsd = computeJSD(currentShare, baselineShare);
109:   const maxShareDrift = Object.values(shareDrift).reduce(
110:     (max, value) => Math.max(max, Math.abs(value)),
111:     0,
112:   );
113: 
114:   return {
115:     share_drift: shareDrift,
116:     psi,
117:     jsd,
118:     flagged: psi >= EDGE_DRIFT_PSI_THRESHOLD
119:       || jsd >= EDGE_DRIFT_JSD_THRESHOLD
120:       || maxShareDrift >= EDGE_DRIFT_SHARE_THRESHOLD,
121:   };
122: }
123: 
124: function getVerificationPassPolicy(
125:   verification: object | null,
126: ): VerificationPassPolicy | undefined {
127:   if (!isRecord(verification) || !isRecord(verification.pass_policy)) {
128:     return undefined;
129:   }
130: 
131:   const { overall_pass_rate, edge_focus_pass_rate } = verification.pass_policy;
132:   if (
133:     typeof overall_pass_rate !== 'number'
134:     || typeof edge_focus_pass_rate !== 'number'
135:   ) {
136:     return undefined;
137:   }
138: 
139:   return {
140:     overall_pass_rate,
141:     edge_focus_pass_rate,
142:   };
143: }
144: 
145: function getVerificationTimestampMs(
146:   verification: object | null,
147:   lastPersistedAt: string | null,
148: ): number | null {
149:   if (isRecord(verification)) {
150:     const persistedTimestamp = verification.verifiedAt ?? verification.executedAt;
151:     if (typeof persistedTimestamp === 'string') {
152:       const parsedPersistedTimestamp = Date.parse(persistedTimestamp);
153:       if (!Number.isNaN(parsedPersistedTimestamp)) {
154:         return parsedPersistedTimestamp;
155:       }
156:     }
157:   }
158: 
159:   if (typeof lastPersistedAt !== 'string') {
160:     return null;
161:   }
162: 
163:   const parsedLastPersistedAt = Date.parse(lastPersistedAt);
164:   return Number.isNaN(parsedLastPersistedAt) ? null : parsedLastPersistedAt;
165: }
166: 
167: function getGoldVerificationTrust(
168:   verification: object | null,
169:   freshness: ReturnType<typeof getGraphFreshness>,
170:   lastPersistedAt: string | null,
171: ): GoldVerificationTrust {
172:   if (!verification) {
173:     return 'absent';
174:   }
175: 
176:   if (freshness !== 'fresh') {
177:     return 'stale';
178:   }
179: 
180:   const verificationTimestampMs = getVerificationTimestampMs(verification, lastPersistedAt);
181:   if (verificationTimestampMs === null) {
182:     return 'stale';
183:   }
184: 
185:   if (Date.now() - verificationTimestampMs > GOLD_VERIFICATION_MAX_AGE_MS) {
186:     return 'stale';
187:   }
188: 
189:   if (isRecord(verification) && verification.passed === false) {
190:     return 'stale';
191:   }
192: 
193:   return 'live';
194: }
195: 
196: /** Handle code_graph_status tool call */
197: export async function handleCodeGraphStatus(): Promise<{ content: Array<{ type: string; text: string }> }> {
198:   // Read the readiness snapshot FIRST so the degraded envelope still surfaces
199:   // even when `graphDb.getStats()` throws (e.g. DB file corrupted or locked).
200:   // Previously stats was called first; on crash the catch path returned a
201:   // generic "Code graph not initialized" error and action-level readiness was
202:   // hidden. The snapshot helper is read-only, so calling it earlier never
203:   // causes side effects.
204:   // DR-001-01: these reads were previously unguarded, so a corrupt/locked DB threw a generic
205:   // error instead of a degraded envelope. Wrap them and surface the same degraded shape.
206:   let snapshot: ReturnType<typeof getGraphReadinessSnapshot>;
207:   let storedScope: ReturnType<typeof graphDb.getStoredCodeGraphScope>;
208:   let activeScopePolicy: ReturnType<typeof resolveIndexScopePolicy>;
209:   try {
210:     snapshot = getGraphReadinessSnapshot(process.cwd());
211:     storedScope = graphDb.getStoredCodeGraphScope();
212:     activeScopePolicy = parseIndexScopePolicyFromFingerprint(storedScope) ?? resolveIndexScopePolicy();
213:   } catch (err: unknown) {
214:     const readinessError = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
215:     return {
216:       content: [{
217:         type: 'text',
218:         text: JSON.stringify({
219:           status: 'error',
220:           message: `code_graph_status_degraded: readiness snapshot unavailable: ${readinessError}`,
221:           data: {
222:             degraded: true,
223:             graphAnswersOmitted: true,
224:             blockReason: 'readiness_unavailable',
225:             fallbackDecision: { nextTool: 'rg', reason: 'readiness_unavailable' },
226:           },
227:         }, null, 2),
228:       }],
229:     };
230:   }
231:   const freshness = snapshot.freshness;
232:   const scopeMismatch = storedScope.fingerprint !== activeScopePolicy.fingerprint;
233: 
234:   // Stats is isolated so an unavailable DB never suppresses the readiness
235:   // snapshot. On failure we ship the snapshot + degraded envelope and
236:   // surface stats-derived fields as null/empty fallbacks.
237:   let stats: ReturnType<typeof graphDb.getStats> | null = null;
238:   let statsError: string | null = null;
239:   try {
240:     stats = graphDb.getStats();
241:   } catch (err: unknown) {
242:     statsError = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
243:   }
244: 
245:   if (!stats) {
246:     // DB unavailable: surface the readiness snapshot + clear recovery signal
247:     // (`rg` fallback per shared degraded-readiness vocabulary) instead of
248:     // returning a generic init error string.
249:     const readinessReason = snapshot.reason && snapshot.reason.length > 0
250:       ? snapshot.reason
251:       : (statsError ? `code-graph stats unavailable: ${statsError}` : 'code-graph stats unavailable');
252:     const readinessBlock = buildReadinessBlock({
253:       freshness,
254:       action: snapshot.action,
255:       inlineIndexPerformed: false,
256:       reason: readinessReason,
257:     });
258:     return {
259:       content: [{
260:         type: 'text',
261:         text: JSON.stringify({
262:           status: 'error',
263:           message: `code_graph_not_initialized: ${statsError ?? 'stats unavailable'}`,
264:           data: {
265:             degraded: true,
266:             graphAnswersOmitted: true,
267:             blockReason: 'stats_unavailable',
268:             readiness: readinessBlock,
269:             canonicalReadiness: readinessBlock.canonicalReadiness,
270:             trustState: readinessBlock.trustState,
271:             fallbackDecision: { nextTool: 'rg', reason: 'stats_unavailable' },
272:           },
273:         }, null, 2),
274:       }],
275:     };
276:   }
277: 
278:   try {
279:     const edgeDriftSummary = buildEdgeDriftSummary(stats.edgesByType);
280:     const lastGoldVerification = graphDb.getLastGoldVerification();
281:     const parseDiagnostics = getParseDiagnosticsForStatus();
282:     const staleButValidGraphFiles = getStaleButValidGraphFilesForStatus();
283:     const lastFailedScan = getLastFailedScanForStatus();
284:     const parserSkipList = getSkipListSummary();
285:     const parserHealth = getParserHealth();
286:     const apply = getLastApplyMetadata();
287:     const goldVerificationTrust = getGoldVerificationTrust(
288:       lastGoldVerification,
289:       freshness,
290:       stats.lastScanTimestamp,
291:     );
292:     const verificationPassPolicy = getVerificationPassPolicy(lastGoldVerification);
293:     // Switch on the unified freshness enum so each canonical state has its own
294:     // status reason (no string-mapping that swallows 'error' as 'missing').
295:     // Prefer the snapshot reason when present (it carries action-level detail
296:     // like "graph is empty (0 nodes)" or "<N> stale files exceed selective
297:     // threshold"); fall back to the canonical state reason otherwise.
298:     let statusReason: string;
299:     switch (freshness) {
300:       case 'fresh':
301:         statusReason = 'status probe reports graph is ready';
302:         break;
303:       case 'stale':
304:         statusReason = 'status probe reports graph is stale';
305:         break;
306:       case 'empty':
307:         statusReason = 'status probe reports graph is missing';
308:         break;
309:       case 'error':
310:         statusReason = 'status probe crashed; graph is unavailable';
311:         break;
312:     }
313:     const readinessReason = snapshot.reason && snapshot.reason.length > 0
314:       ? snapshot.reason
315:       : statusReason;
316:     const readinessBlock = buildReadinessBlock({
317:       freshness,
318:       action: snapshot.action,
319:       inlineIndexPerformed: false,
320:       reason: readinessReason,
321:     });
322: 
323:     return {
324:       content: [{
325:         type: 'text',
326:         text: JSON.stringify({
327:           status: 'ok',
328:           data: {
329:             totalFiles: stats.totalFiles,
330:             totalNodes: stats.totalNodes,
331:             totalEdges: stats.totalEdges,
332:             freshness,
333:             activeScope: {
334:               fingerprint: activeScopePolicy.fingerprint,
335:               label: activeScopePolicy.label,
336:               includeSkills: activeScopePolicy.includeSkills,
337:               includedSkills: activeScopePolicy.includedSkillsList,
338:               includedAgents: activeScopePolicy.includeAgents ? 'all' : 'none',
339:               includedCommands: activeScopePolicy.includeCommands ? 'all' : 'none',
340:               includedSpecs: activeScopePolicy.includeSpecs ? 'all' : 'none',
341:               includedPlugins: activeScopePolicy.includePlugins ? 'all' : 'none',
342:               includeAgents: activeScopePolicy.includeAgents,
343:               includeCommands: activeScopePolicy.includeCommands,
344:               includeSpecs: activeScopePolicy.includeSpecs,
345:               includePlugins: activeScopePolicy.includePlugins,
346:               source: activeScopePolicy.source,
347:             },
348:             storedScope,
349:             scopeMismatch,
350:             excludedTrackedFiles: activeScopePolicy.includedSkillsList === 'all'
351:               ? 0
352:               : graphDb.countTrackedSkillFiles(),
353:             readiness: readinessBlock,
354:             canonicalReadiness: readinessBlock.canonicalReadiness,
355:             trustState: readinessBlock.trustState,
356:             lastScanAt: stats.lastScanTimestamp,
357:             lastPersistedAt: stats.lastScanTimestamp,
358:             lastGitHead: stats.lastGitHead,
359:             dbFileSize: stats.dbFileSize,
360:             schemaVersion: stats.schemaVersion,
361:             nodesByKind: stats.nodesByKind,
362:             edgesByType: stats.edgesByType,
363:             edgeDriftSummary,
364:             parseHealth: stats.parseHealthSummary,
365:             parserHealth,
366:             parseDiagnostics,
367:             parserSkipList,
368:             staleButValidGraphFiles,
369:             ...(lastFailedScan ? { lastFailedScan } : {}),
370:             graphQualitySummary: stats.graphQualitySummary,
371:             goldVerificationTrust,
372:             ...(lastGoldVerification ? { lastGoldVerification } : {}),
373:             ...(verificationPassPolicy ? { verificationPassPolicy } : {}),
374:             apply,
375:           },
376:         }, null, 2),
377:       }],
378:     };
379:   } catch (err: unknown) {
380:     // Preserve the readiness snapshot when the post-stats path fails (e.g.
381:     // drift summary / verification trust calculation throws).
382:     // Operators must still see action-level readiness instead of a generic
383:     // init error. Falls back to the snapshot we computed at the top of the
384:     // handler, which is read-only and never throws (returns 'error'
385:     // freshness if the underlying probe crashes).
386:     const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
387:     const readinessReason = snapshot.reason && snapshot.reason.length > 0
388:       ? snapshot.reason
389:       : `status path failed: ${message}`;
390:     const readinessBlock = buildReadinessBlock({
391:       freshness,
392:       action: snapshot.action,
393:       inlineIndexPerformed: false,
394:       reason: readinessReason,
395:     });
396:     return {
397:       content: [{
398:         type: 'text',
399:         text: JSON.stringify({
400:           status: 'error',
401:           message: `code_graph_not_initialized: ${message}`,
402:           data: {
403:             degraded: true,
404:             graphAnswersOmitted: true,
405:             blockReason: 'status_path_failed',
406:             readiness: readinessBlock,
407:             canonicalReadiness: readinessBlock.canonicalReadiness,
408:             trustState: readinessBlock.trustState,
409:             fallbackDecision: { nextTool: 'rg', reason: 'status_path_failed' },
410:           },
411:         }, null, 2),
412:       }],
413:     };
414:   }
415: }

(End of file - total 415 lines)
</content>

<content>
1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Code Graph Verify Handler
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // MCP tool handler for code_graph_verify — executes the persisted
5: // gold-query battery against the current code graph.
6: 
7: import { realpathSync } from 'node:fs';
8: import { isAbsolute, relative, resolve, sep } from 'node:path';
9: 
10: import * as graphDb from '../lib/code-graph-db.js';
11: import {
12:   DEFAULT_GOLD_BATTERY_PATH,
13:   executeBattery,
14:   loadGoldBattery,
15:   type GoldBattery,
16: } from '../lib/gold-query-verifier.js';
17: import { ensureCodeGraphReady } from '../lib/ensure-ready.js';
18: import { buildReadinessBlock } from '../lib/readiness-contract.js';
19: import { resolveIndexScopePolicy, scopeFingerprintsMatchOrLegacy } from '../lib/index-scope-policy.js';
20: import { canonicalizeWorkspacePaths, isWithinWorkspace } from '../lib/utils/workspace-path.js';
21: import { handleCodeGraphQuery } from './query.js';
22: 
23: export type VerifyCategory =
24:   | 'mcp-tool'
25:   | 'cross-module'
26:   | 'exported-type'
27:   | 'regression-detection';
28: 
29: export interface VerifyArgs {
30:   rootDir?: string;
31:   batteryPath?: string;
32:   category?: VerifyCategory;
33:   failFast?: boolean;
34:   includeDetails?: boolean;
35:   persistBaseline?: boolean;
36:   allowInlineIndex?: boolean;
37: }
38: 
39: const GOLD_BATTERY_FILENAME = 'code-graph-gold-queries.json';
40: 
41: function buildResponse(payload: object): { content: Array<{ type: 'text'; text: string }> } {
42:   return {
43:     content: [{
44:       type: 'text',
45:       text: JSON.stringify(payload, null, 2),
46:     }],
47:   };
48: }
49: 
50: function scopeDiagnostic(scope: {
51:   fingerprint?: string | null;
52:   label?: string | null;
53:   source?: string | null;
54: }) {
55:   return {
56:     fingerprint: scope.fingerprint ?? null,
57:     label: scope.label ?? null,
58:     source: scope.source ?? null,
59:   };
60: }
61: 
62: function buildScopePreflight() {
63:   const activeScope = resolveIndexScopePolicy();
64:   const storedScope = graphDb.getStoredCodeGraphScope();
65:   const matches = scopeFingerprintsMatchOrLegacy(storedScope.fingerprint, activeScope.fingerprint);
66:   return {
67:     status: matches ? 'pass' : 'mismatch',
68:     activeScope: scopeDiagnostic(activeScope),
69:     storedScope: scopeDiagnostic(storedScope),
70:     reason: matches
71:       ? 'active scope matches stored graph scope'
72:       : 'active scope differs from stored graph scope',
73:   };
74: }
75: 
76: // Informational scope-mismatch field derived from the
77: // already-computed preflight. Returns `null` when scopes match.
78: // When they differ, surfaces the canonical { stored, active,
79: // recommendation } shape so operators can decide whether to
80: // rescan with matching scope or pass `forceScopeChange`. The
81: // field is purely informational — verify proceeds either way.
82: function buildScopeMismatchInfo(
83:   preflight: ReturnType<typeof buildScopePreflight>,
84: ): {
85:   stored: ReturnType<typeof scopeDiagnostic>;
86:   active: ReturnType<typeof scopeDiagnostic>;
87:   recommendation: string;
88: } | null {
89:   if (preflight.status !== 'mismatch') {
90:     return null;
91:   }
92:   return {
93:     stored: preflight.storedScope,
94:     active: preflight.activeScope,
95:     recommendation: 'rescan with matching scope or pass forceScopeChange',
96:   };
97: }
98: 
99: function applyCategoryFilter(
100:   battery: GoldBattery,
101:   category: VerifyCategory | undefined,
102: ): GoldBattery {
103:   if (!category) {
104:     return battery;
105:   }
106: 
107:   const filteredQueries = battery.queries.filter((query) => query.category === category);
108:   if (filteredQueries.length === 0) {
109:     throw new Error(`Gold battery does not contain any queries for category "${category}"`);
110:   }
111: 
112:   return {
113:     ...battery,
114:     queries: filteredQueries,
115:   };
116: }
117: 
118: function isAllowlistedBatteryPath(canonicalWorkspace: string, canonicalBatteryPath: string): boolean {
119:   const allowlistedBases = [
120:     resolve(canonicalWorkspace, '.opencode/specs'),
121:     resolve(canonicalWorkspace, '.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server'),
122:     resolve(canonicalWorkspace, '.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server'),
123:   ];
124: 
125:   return allowlistedBases.some((basePath) => {
126:     const candidateRelative = relative(basePath, canonicalBatteryPath);
127:     if (
128:       candidateRelative.length === 0
129:       || candidateRelative.startsWith('..')
130:       || isAbsolute(candidateRelative)
131:     ) {
132:       return false;
133:     }
134: 
135:     const segments = candidateRelative.split(sep);
136:     const assetDir = segments[segments.length - 2];
137:     const fileName = segments[segments.length - 1];
138:     return segments.length >= 2
139:       && assetDir === 'assets'
140:       && fileName === GOLD_BATTERY_FILENAME;
141:   });
142: }
143: 
144: type ResolveVerifyPathsResult =
145:   | { readonly ok: true; readonly canonicalRootDir: string; readonly canonicalBatteryPath: string }
146:   | { readonly ok: false; readonly error: string };
147: 
148: function resolveVerifyPaths(args: VerifyArgs): ResolveVerifyPathsResult {
149:   const resolvedRootDir = resolve(args.rootDir ?? process.cwd());
150:   const resolvedBatteryPath = resolve(args.batteryPath ?? DEFAULT_GOLD_BATTERY_PATH);
151: 
152:   const canonical = canonicalizeWorkspacePaths(resolvedRootDir);
153:   if (!canonical) {
154:     return {
155:       ok: false,
156:       error: `rootDir path is invalid or contains a broken symlink: ${resolvedRootDir}`,
157:     };
158:   }
159:   const { canonicalWorkspace, canonicalRootDir } = canonical;
160: 
161:   if (!isWithinWorkspace(canonicalWorkspace, canonicalRootDir)) {
162:     return {
163:       ok: false,
164:       error: `rootDir must stay within the workspace root: ${canonicalWorkspace}`,
165:     };
166:   }
167: 
168:   let canonicalBatteryPath: string;
169:   try {
170:     canonicalBatteryPath = realpathSync(resolvedBatteryPath);
171:   } catch {
172:     return {
173:       ok: false,
174:       error: `batteryPath is invalid or contains a broken symlink: ${resolvedBatteryPath}`,
175:     };
176:   }
177: 
178:   if (!isAllowlistedBatteryPath(canonicalWorkspace, canonicalBatteryPath)) {
179:     return {
180:       ok: false,
181:       error: `batteryPath must stay within approved code-graph asset roots under: ${canonicalWorkspace}`,
182:     };
183:   }
184: 
185:   return {
186:     ok: true,
187:     canonicalRootDir,
188:     canonicalBatteryPath,
189:   };
190: }
191: 
192: export async function handleCodeGraphVerify(
193:   args: VerifyArgs,
194: ): Promise<{ content: { type: 'text'; text: string }[] }> {
195:   const paths = resolveVerifyPaths(args);
196:   if (!paths.ok) {
197:     return buildResponse({
198:       status: 'error',
199:       error: `code_graph_verify failed: ${paths.error}`,
200:     });
201:   }
202:   const { canonicalRootDir, canonicalBatteryPath } = paths;
203: 
204:   try {
205:     const readyState = await ensureCodeGraphReady(canonicalRootDir, {
206:       allowInlineIndex: args.allowInlineIndex ?? false,
207:       allowInlineFullScan: false,
208:     });
209:     const readiness = buildReadinessBlock(readyState);
210:     const scopePreflight = buildScopePreflight();
211:     // Informational scopeMismatch field derived from the
212:     // single preflight call (no extra DB query). Surfaces stored
213:     // vs active scope and a remediation hint when the two differ.
214:     const scopeMismatch = buildScopeMismatchInfo(scopePreflight);
215: 
216:     if (readyState.freshness !== 'fresh') {
217:       return buildResponse({
218:         status: 'blocked',
219:         readiness,
220:         scopePreflight,
221:         ...(scopeMismatch ? { scopeMismatch } : {}),
222:       });
223:     }
224: 
225:     // Scope mismatch is informational only — verify proceeds
226:     // and the `scopeMismatch` field on the response surfaces the
227:     // canonical { stored, active, recommendation } shape so operators
228:     // can decide whether to rescan or override. The legacy block
229:     // branch was removed; readiness is now the only blocking signal.
230: 
231:     const battery = applyCategoryFilter(loadGoldBattery(canonicalBatteryPath), args.category);
232:     const result = {
233:       ...(await executeBattery(battery, handleCodeGraphQuery, {
234:         failFast: args.failFast,
235:         includeDetails: args.includeDetails,
236:       })),
237:       batteryPath: canonicalBatteryPath,
238:     };
239: 
240:     if (args.persistBaseline === true) {
241:       graphDb.setLastGoldVerification(result);
242:     }
243: 
244:     return buildResponse({
245:       status: 'ok',
246:       scopePreflight,
247:       ...(scopeMismatch ? { scopeMismatch } : {}),
248:       result,
249:     });
250:   } catch (error: unknown) {
251:     return buildResponse({
252:       status: 'error',
253:       error: `code_graph_verify failed: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}`,
254:     });
255:   }
256: }
