110: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
111: // 3. HELPERS
112: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
113: 
114: export class InvalidDatabaseDirError extends Error {
115:   constructor(message: string) {
116:     super(message);
117:     this.name = 'InvalidDatabaseDirError';
118:   }
119: }
120: 
121: function resolveDb(db?: Database.Database): Database.Database {
122:   return db ?? initializeDb();
123: }
124: 
125: function nowIso(): string {
126:   return new Date().toISOString();
127: }
128: 
129: function readJobStatus(db: Database.Database, jobId: string): ReindexJobStatus | null {
130:   ensureJobTable(db);
131:   const row = db.prepare('SELECT status FROM embedder_jobs WHERE id = ?').get(jobId) as
132:     | { status?: ReindexJobStatus }
133:     | undefined;
134:   return row?.status ?? null;
135: }
136: 
137: function getBatchSize(): number {
138:   return parseBoundedEnv('EMBEDDER_REINDEX_BATCH_SIZE', DEFAULT_BATCH_SIZE, MIN_BATCH_SIZE, MAX_BATCH_SIZE);
139: }
140: 
141: function jobFromRow(row: JobRow): ReindexJob {
142:   const { error, ...job } = row;
143:   return error ? { ...job, error } : job;
144: }
145: 
146: function ensureJobTable(db: Database.Database): void {
147:   db.exec(JOB_TABLE_SQL);
148: }
149: 
150: function selectJob(db: Database.Database, jobId: string): ReindexJob | null {
151:   ensureJobTable(db);
152:   const row = db.prepare(`
153:     SELECT ${JOB_SELECT_COLUMNS}
154:     FROM embedder_jobs
155:     WHERE id = ?
156:   `).get(jobId) as JobRow | undefined;
157: 
158:   return row ? jobFromRow(row) : null;
159: }
160: 
161: function selectActiveJob(db: Database.Database): ReindexJob | null {
162:   ensureJobTable(db);
163:   const row = db.prepare(`
164:     SELECT ${JOB_SELECT_COLUMNS}
165:     FROM embedder_jobs
166:     WHERE status IN ('queued', 'running')
167:     ORDER BY started_at DESC, id DESC
168:     LIMIT 1
169:   `).get() as JobRow | undefined;
170: 
171:   return row ? jobFromRow(row) : null;
172: }
173: 
174: function setJobStatus(
175:   db: Database.Database,
176:   jobId: string,
177:   status: ReindexJobStatus,
178:   processed?: number,
179:   error?: string,
180: ): void {
181:   const params = {
182:     jobId,
183:     status,
184:     updatedAt: nowIso(),
185:     processed,
186:     error,
187:   };
188: 
189:   if (typeof processed === 'number' && typeof error === 'string') {
190:     db.prepare(`
191:       UPDATE embedder_jobs
192:       SET status = @status,
193:           updated_at = @updatedAt,
194:           processed = @processed,
195:           error = @error
196:       WHERE id = @jobId
197:     `).run(params);
198:     return;
199:   }
200: 
201:   if (typeof processed === 'number') {
202:     // DR-001-P1-002: a per-batch progress write must NOT clobber a terminal status set
203:     // by a concurrent cancelJob(). The only caller that passes processed without an error
204:     // is the in-loop 'running' progress update, which must only apply while the job is
205:     // still 'running' (or being re-affirmed as 'running'); never resurrect a 'cancelled'
206:     // (or 'completed'/'failed') row back to 'running'. Terminal transitions (completed /
207:     // failed) always carry the other branches and stay unconditional.
208:     if (status === 'running') {
209:       db.prepare(`
210:         UPDATE embedder_jobs
211:         SET status = @status,
212:             updated_at = @updatedAt,
213:             processed = @processed
214:         WHERE id = @jobId
215:           AND status = 'running'
216:       `).run(params);
217:       return;
218:     }
219:     db.prepare(`
220:       UPDATE embedder_jobs
221:       SET status = @status,
222:           updated_at = @updatedAt,
223:           processed = @processed
224:       WHERE id = @jobId
225:     `).run(params);
226:     return;
227:   }
228: 
229:   if (typeof error === 'string') {
230:     db.prepare(`
231:       UPDATE embedder_jobs
232:       SET status = @status,
233:           updated_at = @updatedAt,
234:           error = @error
235:       WHERE id = @jobId
236:     `).run(params);
237:     return;
238:   }
239: 
240:   db.prepare(`
241:     UPDATE embedder_jobs
242:     SET status = @status,
243:         updated_at = @updatedAt
244:     WHERE id = @jobId
245:   `).run(params);
246: }
247: 
248: function selectMemoryBatch(db: Database.Database, offset: number, limit: number): MemoryRow[] {
249:   return db.prepare(`
250:     SELECT id, content_text, title, file_path
251:     FROM memory_index
252:     ORDER BY id ASC
253:     LIMIT ? OFFSET ?
254:   `).all(limit, offset) as MemoryRow[];
255: }
256: 
257: function memoryText(row: MemoryRow): string {
258:   if (typeof row.content_text === 'string' && row.content_text.length > 0) {
259:     return row.content_text;
260:   }
261:   if (typeof row.title === 'string' && row.title.length > 0) {
262:     return row.title;
263:   }
264:   return row.file_path ?? '';
265: }
266: 
267: function getBatchPair(
268:   rows: MemoryRow[],
269:   embeddings: Float32Array[],
270:   index: number,
271: ): { readonly row: MemoryRow; readonly embedding: Float32Array } {
272:   const row = rows[index];
273:   const embedding = embeddings[index];
274:   if (!row || !embedding) {
275:     const missing: string[] = [];
276:     if (!row) missing.push('row=undefined');
277:     if (!embedding) missing.push('embedding=undefined');
278:     throw new Error(
279:       `Embedding batch entry missing at index ${index}: ${missing.join(', ')} ` +
280:         `(rows.length=${rows.length}, embeddings.length=${embeddings.length})`,
281:     );
282:   }
283:   return { row, embedding };
284: }
285: 
286: function writeVectors(
287:   db: Database.Database,
288:   tableName: string,
289:   rows: MemoryRow[],
290:   embeddings: Float32Array[],
291: ): void {
292:   const writeBatch = db.transaction(() => {
293:     const stmt = db.prepare(`
294:       INSERT OR REPLACE INTO ${tableName} (id, vec)
295:       VALUES (?, ?)
296:     `);
297: 
298:     for (let i = 0; i < rows.length; i += 1) {
299:       const { row, embedding } = getBatchPair(rows, embeddings, i);
300:       stmt.run(row.id, to_embedding_buffer(embedding));
301:     }
302:   });
303: 
304:   writeBatch();
305: }
306: 
307: function getDatabaseDir(db: Database.Database): string | null {
308:   const row = (db.prepare('PRAGMA database_list').all() as Array<{ name?: string; file?: string }>)
309:     .find((entry) => entry.name === 'main');
310:   if (!row?.file || row.file === ':memory:') {
311:     return null;
312:   }
313:   return path.dirname(row.file);
314: }
315: 
316: function getDatabasePath(db: Database.Database): string | null {
317:   try {
318:     const row = (db.prepare('PRAGMA database_list').all() as Array<{ name?: string; file?: string }>)
319:       .find((entry) => entry.name === 'main');
320:     if (!row?.file || row.file === ':memory:') {
321:       return null;
322:     }
323:     return row.file;
324:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
325:     return null;
326:   }
327: }
328: 
329: function isClosedDatabaseHandleError(error: unknown): boolean {
330:   const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
331:   return /database connection is not open|closed database|database handle is closed/i.test(message);
332: }
333: 
334: function scheduleJobAfterRestore(jobId: string, dbPath: string | null): void {
335:   setTimeout(() => {
336:     // Do not re-open the database while a checkpoint restore is still in
337:     // progress: initialize_db runs interrupted-restore recovery, which would
338:     // roll back the in-flight restore. Wait for the barrier to clear.
339:     if (getRestoreBarrierStatus()) {
340:       scheduleJobAfterRestore(jobId, dbPath);
341:       return;
342:     }
343:     try {
344:       enqueueJob(dbPath ? initializeDb(dbPath) : initializeDb(), jobId);
345:     } catch (error: unknown) {
346:       logger.warn('embedder reindex restore retry skipped', {
347:         event: 'embedder_reindex_restore_retry_skipped',
348:         jobId,
349:         error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),
350:       });
351:     }
352:   }, RESTORE_REQUEUE_DELAY_MS).unref?.();
353: }
354: 
355: function requireDatabaseDir(db: Database.Database, operation: string): string {
356:   const databaseDir = getDatabaseDir(db);
357:   if (!databaseDir) {
358:     throw new InvalidDatabaseDirError(
359:       `Embedder reindex ${operation} requires a file-backed database; received an in-memory or anonymous database without a database directory`,
360:     );
361:   }
362:   return databaseDir;
363: }
364: 
365: function writeVectorsToKnn(
366:   db: Database.Database,
367:   rows: MemoryRow[],
368:   embeddings: Float32Array[],
369: ): void {
370:   const writeBatch = db.transaction(() => {
371:     const del = db.prepare('DELETE FROM vec_memories WHERE rowid = ?');
372:     const ins = db.prepare('INSERT INTO vec_memories (rowid, embedding) VALUES (?, ?)');
373:     for (let i = 0; i < rows.length; i += 1) {
374:       const { row, embedding } = getBatchPair(rows, embeddings, i);
375:       del.run(BigInt(row.id));
376:       ins.run(BigInt(row.id), to_embedding_buffer(embedding));
377:     }
378:   });
379: 
380:   writeBatch();
381: }
382: 
383: function ensureShardSchema(shard: Database.Database, profile: EmbeddingProfile, tableName: string): void {
384:   shard.exec(`
385:     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS vec_metadata (
386:       key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
387:       value TEXT NOT NULL,
388:       created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
389:     );
390:     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ${tableName} (
391:       id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
392:       vec BLOB NOT NULL
393:     );
394:   `);
395: 
396:   const writeMetadata = shard.transaction(() => {
397:     const stmt = shard.prepare('INSERT OR REPLACE INTO vec_metadata (key, value) VALUES (?, ?)');
398:     stmt.run('provider', profile.provider);
399:     stmt.run('model', profile.model);
400:     stmt.run('dim', String(profile.dim));
401:     stmt.run('embedding_dim', String(profile.dim));
402:   });
403:   writeMetadata();
404: }
405: 
406: function writeVectorsToShard(
407:   db: Database.Database,
408:   profile: EmbeddingProfile,
409:   tableName: string,
410:   rows: MemoryRow[],
411:   embeddings: Float32Array[],
412:   shardPath?: string,
413: ): void {
414:   const databaseDir = requireDatabaseDir(db, 'vector shard write');
415: 
416:   // DR-020: writes target a caller-supplied STAGING shard path (default: the live
417:   // active shard for backward compatibility). runJob writes every batch to the staging
418:   // path and atomically renames staging -> active on success, so a mid-loop failure can
419:   // never leave the live (active) shard half-written.
420:   const shard = new Database(shardPath ?? profile.getVectorShardPath(databaseDir));
421:   try {
422:     shard.pragma('journal_mode = WAL');
423: 
424:     // Load sqlite-vec so the runtime KNN virtual table can be created
425:     // and populated alongside the canonical vec_<dim> blob table. When the extension
426:     // is unavailable the runtime falls back to vec_<dim> only and search degrades to
427:     // lexical signals; we surface that through the existing console.warn in vector-index-store.
428:     let vecAvailable = true;
429:     try {
430:       sqliteVec.load(shard);
431:     } catch (_err) {
432:       vecAvailable = false;
433:     }
434: 
435:     ensureShardSchema(shard, profile, tableName);
436: 
437:     if (vecAvailable) {
438:       shard.exec(`
439:         CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS vec_memories USING vec0(
440:           embedding FLOAT[${profile.dim}]
441:         )
442:       `);
443:     }
444: 
445:     writeVectors(shard, tableName, rows, embeddings);
446:     if (vecAvailable) {
447:       writeVectorsToKnn(shard, rows, embeddings);
448:     }
449:   } finally {
450:     // WAL-durability: flush + TRUNCATE the shard WAL before close so the shard file is
451:     // consistent at rest with an empty WAL — mirrors close_db's corruption-prevention.
452:     // better-sqlite3 .close() only does a passive checkpoint; an abrupt later kill could otherwise
453:     // corrupt un-checkpointed shard frames (vec0 / FTS segment writes). Best-effort; never block close.
454:     try { shard.pragma('wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)'); } catch (_err: unknown) { /* best-effort */ }
455:     shard.close();
456:   }
457: }
458: 
459: function enqueueJob(db: Database.Database, jobId: string): void {
460:   if (runningJobs.has(jobId)) {
461:     return;
462:   }
463: 
464:   runningJobs.add(jobId);
465:   void runJob(db, jobId).finally(() => {
466:     runningJobs.delete(jobId);
467:   });
468: }
469: 
470: async function runJob(db: Database.Database, jobId: string): Promise<void> {
471:   const dbPath = getDatabasePath(db);
472:   let jobDb = db;
473:   if (getRestoreBarrierStatus()) {
474:     scheduleJobAfterRestore(jobId, dbPath);
475:     return;
476:   }
477:   const resolveJobDb = (): Database.Database => {
478:     const barrierStatus = getRestoreBarrierStatus();
479:     if (barrierStatus) {
480:       const error = new Error(barrierStatus.message);
481:       error.name = barrierStatus.code;
482:       throw error;
483:     }
484:     return jobDb;
485:   };
486:   jobDb = resolveJobDb();
487:   const initialJob = selectJob(jobDb, jobId);
488:   if (!initialJob || initialJob.status === 'completed' || initialJob.status === 'failed' || initialJob.status === 'cancelled') {
489:     return;
490:   }
491: 
492:   const manifest = getManifest(initialJob.toName);
493:   if (!manifest) {
494:     setJobStatus(jobDb, jobId, 'failed', undefined, `UNKNOWN_EMBEDDER: ${initialJob.toName}`);
495:     return;
496:   }
497:   const adapter = getEmbedderAdapter(manifest.backend, initialJob.toName, initialJob.toDim);
498:   const targetProfile = new EmbeddingProfile({
499:     provider: manifest.backend,
500:     model: manifest.name,
501:     dim: manifest.dim,
502:     dtype: null,
503:     baseUrl: null,
504:   });
505: 
506:   ensureVecTableForDim(jobDb, initialJob.toDim);
507:   const tableName = vecTableNameForDim(initialJob.toDim);
508:   const batchSize = getBatchSize();
509:   let processed = initialJob.processed;
510: 
511:   // DR-020: stage every shard write into a per-job staging file, then atomically
512:   // rename it over the live active shard ONLY after the completion transaction commits.
513:   // A mid-loop throw (or cancel) leaves the live (active) shard untouched; the partial
514:   // staging artifact is unlinked in the catch/cancel paths. databaseDir is guaranteed
515:   // file-backed here (startReindexInternal already called requireDatabaseDir).
516:   const databaseDir = requireDatabaseDir(jobDb, 'reindex staging');
517:   const activeShardPath = targetProfile.getVectorShardPath(databaseDir);
518:   const stagingShardPath = `${activeShardPath}.reindex-${jobId}.staging`;
519: 
520:   const cleanupStaging = (): void => {
521:     for (const suffix of ['', '-wal', '-shm']) {
522:       try {
523:         fs.unlinkSync(`${stagingShardPath}${suffix}`);
524:       } catch (_err: unknown) {
525:         /* best-effort: staging may not exist */
526:       }
527:     }
528:   };
529: 
530:   try {
531:     setJobStatus(jobDb, jobId, 'running');
532:     // Start each run from a clean staging slate (a prior crashed run may have left one).
533:     cleanupStaging();
534: 
535:     while (processed < initialJob.total) {
536:       jobDb = resolveJobDb();
537:       // DR-001-P1-002: re-read the live cancel/status before each batch so cancelJob()
538:       // during a run stops the worker before the next write. Abort cleanly: drop the
539:       // staging artifact and leave the live shard + active pointer untouched.
540:       if (readJobStatus(jobDb, jobId) === 'cancelled') {
541:         cleanupStaging();
542:         return;
543:       }
544: 
545:       const rows = selectMemoryBatch(jobDb, processed, batchSize);
546:       if (rows.length === 0) {
547:         break;
548:       }
549: 
550:       const embeddings = await adapter.embed(rows.map(memoryText));
551:       jobDb = resolveJobDb();
552:       if (embeddings.length !== rows.length) {
553:         throw new Error(`Embedder returned ${embeddings.length} embeddings for ${rows.length} memories`);
554:       }
555: 
556:       writeVectors(jobDb, tableName, rows, embeddings);
557:       writeVectorsToShard(jobDb, targetProfile, tableName, rows, embeddings, stagingShardPath);
558:       processed += rows.length;
559:       // Clobber-safe: only advances while status is still 'running' (never resurrects
560:       // a concurrently-set 'cancelled').
561:       setJobStatus(jobDb, jobId, 'running', processed);
562:       await new Promise<void>((resolve) => {
563:         setImmediate(resolve);
564:       });
565:     }
566: 
567:     // DR-001-P1-002: final cancel checkpoint before the irreversible swap/commit.
568:     jobDb = resolveJobDb();
569:     if (readJobStatus(jobDb, jobId) === 'cancelled') {
570:       cleanupStaging();
571:       return;
572:     }
573: 
574:     // DR-020: atomically swap the staged shard over the live active shard BEFORE the
575:     // completion transaction flips the active-embedder pointer. rename(2) is atomic on
576:     // the same filesystem; the staging file always lives beside the active shard, so the
577:     // active shard either is the old file (failure) or the fully-staged new file (success),
578:     // never a half-written mix. If no batch produced a staging file (e.g. zero rows), skip.
579:     if (fs.existsSync(stagingShardPath)) {
580:       // Drop any stale sidecars next to the active shard so the renamed WAL/SHM (already
581:       // TRUNCATE-checkpointed at staging close) is the authoritative pair.
582:       for (const suffix of ['-wal', '-shm']) {
583:         try {
584:           fs.unlinkSync(`${activeShardPath}${suffix}`);
585:         } catch (_err: unknown) {
586:           /* best-effort */
587:         }
588:       }
589:       fs.renameSync(stagingShardPath, activeShardPath);
590:     }
591: 
592:     const complete = jobDb.transaction(() => {
593:       // Persist the active-embedder provider pointer too; omitting it leaves the pointer empty,
594:       // which readActiveEmbedderIfValid coerces to undefined and the next boot loses provider
595:       // identity. 'api' backends are ambiguous (openai vs voyage) from the manifest alone, so
596:       // leave the provider unset for those rather than guess.
597:       const activeProvider = manifest.backend === 'ollama'
598:         ? 'ollama'
599:         : manifest.backend === 'sentence-transformers'
600:           ? 'hf-local'
601:           : undefined;
602:       setActiveEmbedder(jobDb, initialJob.toName, initialJob.toDim, activeProvider);
603:       // Commit embedding_status for rows now backed by an active-profile vector.
604:       // A vector-only reindex previously wrote vectors but left memory_index.embedding_status
605:       // stale, so a "completed" bulk re-embed never raised the success count. Reconcile the
606:       // status for every row present in the just-written vec_<dim> table, inside the same
607:       // atomic completion transaction so vectors and status flip together.
608:       const completedAt = nowIso();
609:       jobDb.prepare(`
610:         UPDATE memory_index
611:         SET embedding_status = 'success',
612:             embedding_generated_at = COALESCE(embedding_generated_at, @ts),
613:             updated_at = @ts,
614:             failure_reason = NULL
615:         WHERE embedding_status != 'success'
616:           AND id IN (SELECT id FROM ${tableName})
617:       `).run({ ts: completedAt });
618:       setJobStatus(jobDb, jobId, 'completed', initialJob.total);
619:     });
620:     complete();
621:     // The active-embedder pointer just flipped; drop the cached provider singleton so the
622:     // next embedding re-resolves against the new pointer instead of the stale model/dim.
623:     invalidateProviderSingleton();
624:     void teardownEmbedderAfterSwap(manifest.backend, initialJob.toName).catch((error: unknown) => {
625:       console.warn(`[embedder-reindex] embedder teardown after swap failed: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}`);
626:     });
627:     if (getDatabaseDir(jobDb)) {
628:       attachActiveVectorShard(jobDb, targetProfile);
629:     }
630:   } catch (error: unknown) {
631:     if (getRestoreBarrierStatus() || isClosedDatabaseHandleError(error)) {
632:       cleanupStaging();
633:       scheduleJobAfterRestore(jobId, dbPath);
634:       logger.warn('embedder reindex paused for checkpoint restore', {
635:         event: 'embedder_reindex_restore_paused',
636:         jobId,
637:         processed,
638:       });
639:       return;
640:     }
641:     // DR-020: a failed run must not leave a half-written shard anywhere. The live active
642:     // shard was never mutated (all writes went to staging); discard the partial staging
643:     // artifact so the next attempt starts clean and the active shard stays the last-good one.
644:     cleanupStaging();
645:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
646:     setJobStatus(jobDb, jobId, 'failed', processed, message);
647:     logger.error('embedder reindex job failed', {
648:       event: 'embedder_reindex_failed',
649:       jobId,
650:       toName: initialJob.toName,
651:       processed,
652:       total: initialJob.total,
653:       error: message,
654:     });
655:   }
656: }
657: 
658: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
659: // 4. CORE LOGIC
660: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
661: 
662: export function startReindex(
663:   options: StartReindexOptions,
664:   runtimeOptions: ReindexRuntimeOptions = {},
665: ): string {
666:   return startReindexInternal(options, runtimeOptions);
667: }
668: 
669: function startReindexInternal(
670:   options: StartReindexOptions,
671:   runtimeOptions: ReindexInternalRuntimeOptions = {},
672: ): string {
673:   const db = resolveDb(runtimeOptions.db);
674:   requireDatabaseDir(db, 'startup');
675:   ensureJobTable(db);
676: 
677:   const manifest = getManifest(options.toName);
678:   if (!manifest) {
679:     const error = new Error(`UNKNOWN_EMBEDDER: ${options.toName}`);
680:     error.name = 'UNKNOWN_EMBEDDER';
681:     throw error;
682:   }
683: 
684:   ensureVecTableForDim(db, manifest.dim);
685:   const active = getActiveEmbedder(db);
686:   const totalRow = db.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM memory_index').get() as { count?: number } | undefined;
687:   const total = Number(totalRow?.count ?? 0);
688:   const timestamp = nowIso();
689:   const id = `emb-swap-${timestamp.replace(/[:.]/g, '-')}-${randomUUID().slice(0, 8)}`;
690: 
691:   db.prepare(`
692:     INSERT INTO embedder_jobs (
693:       id, from_name, to_name, to_dim, total, processed, status, started_at, updated_at, error
694:     ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 0, 'queued', ?, ?, NULL)
695:   `).run(id, active.name, manifest.name, manifest.dim, total, timestamp, timestamp);
696: 
697:   if (runtimeOptions.autoStart !== false) {
698:     enqueueJob(db, id);
699:   }
700: 
701:   return id;
702: }
703: 
704: export function getJobStatus(jobId: string, db?: Database.Database): ReindexJob | null {
705:   return selectJob(resolveDb(db), jobId);
706: }
707: 
708: export function getActiveJob(db?: Database.Database): ReindexJob | null {
709:   return selectActiveJob(resolveDb(db));
710: }
711: 
712: export function cancelJob(jobId: string, db?: Database.Database): ReindexJob | null {
713:   const resolvedDb = resolveDb(db);
714:   ensureJobTable(resolvedDb);
715:   const job = selectJob(resolvedDb, jobId);
716:   if (!job) {
717:     return null;
718:   }
719: 
720:   if (job.status === 'completed' || job.status === 'failed' || job.status === 'cancelled') {
721:     return job;
722:   }
723: 
724:   setJobStatus(resolvedDb, jobId, 'cancelled');
725:   return selectJob(resolvedDb, jobId);
726: }
727: 
728: export function resumeReindexJobs(db?: Database.Database): ReindexJob[] {
729:   const resolvedDb = resolveDb(db);
730:   requireDatabaseDir(resolvedDb, 'resume');
731:   ensureJobTable(resolvedDb);
732:   const rows = resolvedDb.prepare(`
733:     SELECT ${JOB_SELECT_COLUMNS}
734:     FROM embedder_jobs
735:     WHERE status IN ('queued', 'running')
736:     ORDER BY started_at ASC, id ASC
737:   `).all() as JobRow[];
738: 
739:   const jobs = rows.map(jobFromRow);
740:   for (const job of jobs) {
741:     enqueueJob(resolvedDb, job.id);
742:   }
743:   return jobs;
744: }
745: 
746: export function startReindexForTest(
747:   options: StartReindexOptions,
748:   runtimeOptions: ReindexInternalRuntimeOptions = {},
749: ): string {
750:   return startReindexInternal(options, runtimeOptions);
751: }
752: 
753: export function estimateEta(job: ReindexJob | null): number | null {
754:   if (!job || job.status === 'completed') {
755:     return 0;
756:   }
757:   if (job.processed <= 0 || job.total <= 0) {
758:     return null;
759:   }
760: 
761:   const elapsedMs = Date.now() - Date.parse(job.startedAt);
762:   if (!Number.isFinite(elapsedMs) || elapsedMs <= 0) {
763:     return null;
764:   }
765: 
766:   const ratePerMs = job.processed / elapsedMs;
767:   if (ratePerMs <= 0) {
768:     return null;
769:   }
770: 
771:   return Math.ceil((job.total - job.processed) / ratePerMs / 1000);
772: }

1: // ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Memory Embedding Reconcile
3: // ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // One-time/idempotent maintenance that converges memory_index.embedding_status
5: // with active vector coverage. A row that already has both active vector
6: // surfaces (vec_memories_rowids + vec_<dim>) must not stay failed/pending/retry.
7: //
8: // Safety model: dry-run is the default. The active shard is resolved from
9: // runtime metadata only (never a caller-supplied path) and verified against the
10: // main active-embedder pointer before any read or mutation; requireActiveShard
11: // fails closed on mismatch.
12: 
13: import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';
14: 
15: /** Maintenance modes. */
16: export type EmbeddingReconcileMode = 'dry-run' | 'apply';
17: 
18: /** Arguments for memory_embedding_reconcile. Dry-run defaults across the board. */
19: export interface EmbeddingReconcileArgs {
20:   mode?: EmbeddingReconcileMode;
21:   /**
22:    * Reserved / no-op. The active shard is ALWAYS resolved and verified from
23:    * runtime metadata; this flag has no runtime effect and is retained only for
24:    * contract stability.
25:    */
26:   activeOnly?: boolean;
27:   /** Reset genuinely missing-vector retention failures to retry-eligible (default true). */
28:   resetMissing?: boolean;
29:   /** Which missing-vector failures may be reset. Only retention failures by default. */
30:   missingFailureScope?: 'retry-retention';
31:   /** Masked-duplicate failed rows are reconciled to success, never pruned, here. */
32:   maskedFailedPolicy?: 'reconcile';
33:   /** Non-retention provider failures are reported, not mutated, by default. */
34:   providerFailurePolicy?: 'report-only';
35:   /** Fail closed when the active shard cannot be verified (default true). */
36:   requireActiveShard?: boolean;
37:   /**
38:    * Also reset `success` rows that are MISSING an active vector
39:    * surface back to `retry` so the retry-manager re-embeds them. Default false;
40:    * the count is always reported under `coverage` regardless.
41:    */
42:   repairSuccessCoverage?: boolean;
43: }
44: 
45: export interface ReconcileActiveEmbedder {
46:   provider: string | null;
47:   name: string | null;
48:   dim: number | null;
49:   shard: string | null;
50: }
51: 
52: export interface ReconcileSafety {
53:   activeShardVerified: boolean;
54:   vectorPresenceSource: string;
55:   dimensionTableChecked: string | null;
56:   /** Present only when verification failed — explains why. */

58: }

62:   pending: number;
63:   retry: number;
64: }

73:     policy: string;
74:   };
75: }

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: IPC Socket Server (multi-client launcher bridge)
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Canonical bridge logic shared by every daemon launcher (memory,
5: // code-index, skill-advisor). Each service supplies its own socket/db
6: // PATHS; only the bind/reclaim/serve LOGIC lives here so the security
7: // and race-safety contract stays identical across services.
8: 
9: import fs from 'node:fs';
10: import net from 'node:net';
11: import os from 'node:os';
12: import path from 'node:path';
13: 
14: import { StdioServerTransport } from '@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/stdio.js';
15: 
16: const SOCKET_FILE_NAME = 'daemon-ipc.sock';
17: const DEFAULT_MAX_SECONDARY_CLIENTS = 8;
18: const TCP_EADDRINUSE_RETRY_DELAYS_MS = [100, 250, 500, 1000, 1500] as const;
19: 
20: interface IpcBridgeStats {
21:   socket_path: string | null;
22:   secondary_clients_count: number;
23:   total_secondary_messages_in: number;
24:   total_secondary_messages_out: number;
25: }
26: 
27: // Structural shape of the only MCP server capability this bridge uses. Typed structurally (a
28: // method, so its transport param is bivariant) rather than against a concrete `Server` import so
29: // every consuming package satisfies it with its own `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` copy — the three
30: // daemons each install the SDK separately and its private members make the classes nominally
31: // distinct, which a concrete import would reject across package boundaries.
32: interface McpServerLike {
33:   connect(transport: StdioServerTransport): Promise<void>;
34: }
35: 
36: interface IpcSocketServerOptions {
37:   readonly socketPath: string;
38:   readonly createServer: () => McpServerLike;
39:   readonly maxClients?: number;
40:   readonly log?: (message: string) => void;
41:   readonly onActivity?: () => void;
42: }
43: 
44: interface IpcSocketServerHandle {
45:   readonly socketPath: string;
46:   readonly close: () => Promise<void>;
47: }
48: 
49: let activeServer: net.Server | null = null;
50: let activeSocketPath: string | null = null;
51: const activeSockets = new Set<net.Socket>();
52: const activeTransports = new Map<net.Socket, StdioServerTransport>();
53: let totalSecondaryMessagesIn = 0;
54: let totalSecondaryMessagesOut = 0;
55: 
56: function countJsonRpcFrames(chunk: unknown): number {
57:   const text = Buffer.isBuffer(chunk) ? chunk.toString('utf8') : String(chunk ?? '');
58:   const newlineCount = text.split('\n').length - 1;
59:   return Math.max(1, newlineCount);
60: }

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Config
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: 
5: import { existsSync, mkdirSync } from 'node:fs';
6: import { basename, dirname, resolve } from 'node:path';
7: import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';
8: import { resolveCanonicalDbDir } from '../lib/canonical-db-dir.js';
9: 
10: const __filename = fileURLToPath(import.meta.url);
11: const __dirname = dirname(__filename);
12: 
13: const envDir = process.env.SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR;
14: // Default DB dir = the SKILL-LOCAL location `.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/database`
15: // (operator directive 2026-05-29: keep code-graph state inside the skill folder, which every runtime
16: // already shares via the `.opencode/skills` symlink, so a skill-local DB is a single shared instance).
17: // Resolved against the workspace root so it is stable regardless of CWD. `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR`
18: // overrides it. This supersedes the former shared `.opencode/.spec-kit/code-graph/database` location.
19: // (resolveWorkspaceRoot is a hoisted function declaration, safe here.)
20: const defaultDir = resolve(resolveWorkspaceRoot(), '.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/database');
21: 
22: function resolveWorkspaceRoot(): string {
23:   // The server module always lives under `<workspace-root>/.opencode/...`, so
24:   // the workspace root is the parent of the `.opencode` segment on THIS file's
25:   // own ancestry path. Walk up looking for the directory literally named
26:   // `.opencode` and return its parent.
27:   //
28:   // The previous implementation returned the first ancestor that *contained* a
29:   // `.opencode/` child. That falsely matched stray nested `.opencode/` dirs
30:   // sitting as siblings of an ancestor (e.g. an accidental
31:   // `skills/system-code-graph/.opencode/` advisor-state artifact), resolving the
32:   // root to a stray nested skill dir instead of the true workspace root. (The DB dir is
33:   // now skill-local, but resolving the real workspace root still matters for env overrides.)
34:   // Anchoring on the on-path `.opencode` segment is immune to those strays.
35:   let current = __dirname;
36:   for (let i = 0; i < 12; i++) {
37:     if (basename(current) === '.opencode') {
38:       return dirname(current);
39:     }
40:     const parent = dirname(current);
41:     if (parent === current) break;
42:     current = parent;
43:   }
44:   return process.cwd();
45: }
46: 
47: export const DATABASE_DIR = resolveCanonicalDbDir(envDir ?? defaultDir, resolveWorkspaceRoot());
48: 
49: if (!existsSync(DATABASE_DIR)) {
50:   mkdirSync(DATABASE_DIR, { recursive: true });
51: }
